faculty.summary: Co-founder and former VP of Research at Akamai Technologies. Researches distributed systems, content delivery networks, internet routing, and network security. ACM Fellow recognized for contributions to content distribution networks and network theory.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Networks and Distributed Systems Lab. Researches internet architecture, DoS/DDoS defense, internet routing and anycast, network accountability, and web privacy. Developed capability-based DDoS limiting and Sybil detection in social networks.

faculty.name: Matthew Lentz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of systems, networking, and security. Builds abstractions and tools for secure, trustworthy software and improves performance for modern cloud and ML networking. MICRO 2025 Best Paper award for repurposing video codecs as tensor codecs.

faculty.name: Lisa Wu Wills
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the APEX Lab focusing on hardware-software co-design for emerging applications. Designs domain-specific accelerators for genomics, protein discovery, graph analytics, and homomorphic encryption. Also applies deep learning to predict hardware synthesis outcomes.

faculty.name: Michael Reiter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: James B. Duke Distinguished Professor and ACM/IEEE Fellow. Researches computer and communications security including adversarial machine learning, BFT consensus protocols (co-author of HotStuff), and credential abuse prevention. Founded CyLab at Carnegie Mellon.

faculty.name: Kartik Nayak
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches blockchain protocols, applied cryptography, and privacy-preserving computation. Develops efficient Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus protocols (Sync HotStuff, Hydrangea) and threshold decryption schemes. Multiple best paper awards at OSDI and CCS.

faculty.name: Danfeng Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of programming languages and security. Develops tools to automatically prove differential privacy, detect side-channel vulnerabilities, and verify information flow security in software and hardware. NSF CAREER and multiple best paper awards.

faculty.name: Pardis Emami-Naeini
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the InSPIre Lab studying the human side of security and privacy. Created cybersecurity nutrition labels that directly shaped the U.S. Cyber Trust Mark initiative. Researches privacy risks of AI chatbots for mental health and IoT/privacy for vulnerable populations.

faculty.name: Pankaj K. Agarwal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: RJR Nabisco Distinguished Professor researching geometric algorithms, shape analysis, and spatial data structures. Develops efficient algorithms for motion planning, GIS, ecological modeling, sensor networks, and trajectory data mining with applications in multi-robot systems.

faculty.name: Debmalya Panigrahi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Chair of CS. Designs algorithms for graph problems including network flows, graph cuts, and connectivity. Recently broke the 60-year-old cubic runtime barrier for all-pairs max-flows. Also develops learning-augmented online algorithms and approximation schemes.

faculty.name: Kamesh Munagala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops models, algorithms, and markets for resource allocation and decision-making. Researches approximation and online algorithms, computational economics, algorithmic fairness, and deliberative social choice. ACM Distinguished Member and Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Benjamin Rossman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computational complexity theory, especially circuit complexity lower bounds. Proved breakthrough results on monotone circuit lower bounds for k-clique, and established connections between sum-of-squares complexity and algebraic complexity class separations.

faculty.name: John H. Reif
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in biomolecular computation and DNA nanotechnology. Develops autonomous DNA nanomechanical devices, DNA strand displacement circuits, and self-assembled nanostructures. Also contributes to parallel and randomized algorithms, computational complexity, and molecular-scale AI.

faculty.name: Robert Calderbank
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Charles S. Sydnor Distinguished Professor and Director of the Information Initiative at Duke. Co-inventor of space-time codes underpinning modern wireless and the Calderbank-Shor-Steane quantum error-correcting code. Shannon Award and Hamming Medal recipient.

faculty.name: Alexander J. Hartemink
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies machine learning and Bayesian statistics to molecular biology. Develops models for transcriptional regulation, chromatin occupancy (RoboCOP/DynaCOP), nucleosome dynamics during DNA replication, and single-cell multi-omic data integration for gene regulatory network inference.

faculty.name: Eugene Agichtein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on information retrieval, recommender systems, conversational AI, and web search. Leads the Intelligent Information Access Lab.

faculty.name: Dorian Arnold
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on operating and distributed systems, high-performance computing, systems software, and resilience/fault-tolerance.

faculty.name: Yana Bromberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on molecular function prediction from genomic data using computational, machine learning, and network-based methods for annotation and analysis of molecular functions.

faculty.name: Jinho Choi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on natural language processing, machine learning, text mining, and medical informatics. Leads the Emory NLP lab.

faculty.name: Ali Emami
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on NLP, exploring advanced reasoning capabilities in large language models and innovative benchmarking methods.

faculty.name: Michelangelo Grigni
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on theory of computation: approximate subgraph optimization problems, graph decomposition algorithms (spanners and separators), metric approximation.

faculty.name: Joyce Ho
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Studies CS MS
faculty.summary: Research on data mining, machine learning, healthcare informatics, dimensionality reduction, interpretable models, and electronic health records.

faculty.name: Wei Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on graph neural networks, graph machine learning, data-centric AI, trustworthy AI, and AI for science and healthcare.

faculty.name: Joon-Seok Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on data science with emphasis on AI and simulation for urban environments, human mobility, social networks, and public health.

faculty.name: Youngjoong Kwon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computer vision and computer graphics, human digitization, 3D reconstruction, AR/VR, and healthcare applications.

faculty.name: Fei Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on natural language processing and deep learning, including summarization, large language models, and generative AI.

faculty.name: Ken Mandelberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on operating systems, networking, and graphics.

faculty.name: Temiloluwa Prioleau
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on digital and computational health, mobile/wearable technology, and data science/machine learning for human-centered computing and personalized health.

faculty.name: Yolanda Rankin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on human-computer interaction, with interests in CS education and gaming.

faculty.name: Kai Shu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on AI for social good (disinformation, privacy, security, healthcare), trustworthy AI, self-/weakly-supervised learning, and large foundation model factuality and reliability.

faculty.name: Vaidy Sunderam
faculty.title: Professor; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research on concurrent and distributed heterogeneous computing systems, collaborative computing, and software infrastructures for networked data management and computation.

faculty.name: Shengpu Tang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on machine learning, reinforcement learning, healthcare informatics, precision medicine, and electronic health records.

faculty.name: Dishita Turakhia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on HCI and learning sciences, designing systems for learning physical/motor/fabrication skills using ubiquitous sensing, AR/VR/XR tools, and generative AI.

faculty.name: Emily Wall
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on information visualization, visual analytics, decision making, and human-computer interaction.

faculty.name: Kristin Williams
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on HCI, lightweight Internet of Things for the home, backscatter communication, battery-free wireless UI, and upcycled IoT.

faculty.name: Li Xiong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on data privacy and security, data and information management, and biomedical informatics. Leads the AIMS lab.

faculty.name: Carl Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on graph data mining, applied machine learning, biomedical informatics, knowledge bases, social networks, and recommender systems.

faculty.name: Liang Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on data mining, machine learning, and nonconvex optimization, with deep learning on graphs, societal event prediction, spatio-temporal data mining, and interpretable machine learning.

faculty.name: Andreas Z眉fle
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on databases, data mining, simulation, efficient algorithms, and spatial computing.

funding.note: Fully funded first 5 years for students in good standing: tuition, health insurance, service stipend assistantship

faculty.name: Sarah Adel Bargal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; Provost's Distinguished Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Research on computer vision and machine learning, with focus on deep learning for visual understanding.

faculty.name: Matt Blaze
faculty.title: Professor; Robert L. McDevitt Chair
faculty.summary: Research on cryptography, computer and network security, and technology policy.

faculty.name: Jeremy Fineman
faculty.title: Provost's Distinguished Professor; Wagner Term Chair
faculty.summary: Research on algorithm design, data structures, parallel algorithms, scheduling, and memory-efficient algorithms.

faculty.name: Ophir Frieder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on information retrieval, medical informatics, and communication systems.

faculty.name: Sasha Golovnev
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computational complexity, algorithms, pseudorandomness, cryptography, and learning theory.

faculty.name: Bala Kalyanasundaram
faculty.title: Craves Family Professor
faculty.summary: Research on online, parallel, and randomized algorithms, concrete complexity, learning theory, networking, and bioinformatics.

faculty.name: Yoshi Kohno
faculty.title: Professor; Robert L. McDevitt Chair
faculty.summary: Research on computer security, artificial intelligence and society, technology policy, and ethics and technology.

faculty.name: Mark Maloof
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on machine learning, data mining, online learning, concept drift, and security applications of ML.

faculty.name: Calvin Newport
faculty.title: Provost's Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on distributed algorithms and wireless networks.

faculty.name: Kobbi Nissim
faculty.title: Professor; Robert L. McDevitt Term Chair
faculty.summary: Research on cryptography, differential privacy, and privacy law and policy.

faculty.name: Elissa Redmiles
faculty.title: Clare Boothe Luce Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on security, privacy, and ethics, with focus on usable security, measurement, HCI, and data science.

faculty.name: Nathan Schneider
faculty.title: Associate Professor (joint with Linguistics)
faculty.summary: Research on computational linguistics and natural language processing, including corpus annotation, machine learning, and applications.

faculty.name: Micah Sherr
faculty.title: Callahan Family Professor
faculty.summary: Research on security, privacy, and networks. Leads the Georgetown Security Lab.

faculty.name: Clay Shields
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computer security, network security, and digital forensics.

faculty.name: Lisa Singh
faculty.title: Professor; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research on data mining, social mining, privacy, visual analytics, data science, and databases.

faculty.name: Justin Thaler
faculty.title: Provost's Distinguished Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on algorithms for massive data sets, verifiable computation, and computational learning.

faculty.name: Benjamin Ujcich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on security, networking, systems, and provenance.

faculty.name: Nitin Vaidya
faculty.title: Professor; Robert L. McDevitt Chair
faculty.summary: Research on distributed algorithms and systems, secure and fault-tolerant algorithms for optimization and machine learning.

faculty.name: Mahe Velauthapillai
faculty.title: Professor; McBride Family Endowed Chair
faculty.summary: Research on learning theory, algorithms, and bioinformatics.

faculty.name: Radha Venkatagiri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computer architecture, hardware resiliency, and approximate computing.

faculty.name: Muthu Venkitasubramaniam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on cryptography, privacy, network security, and complexity theory.

faculty.name: Grace Hui Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on information retrieval, deep reinforcement learning, machine learning, ontology, question answering, and privacy.

faculty.name: Srinivas Aluru
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in parallel algorithms for computational biology and bioinformatics. Develops high-performance computing methods for genome assembly, sequence analysis, and large-scale biological data processing. Known for foundational contributions to scalable bioinformatics algorithms.

faculty.name: King Jordan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs a computational laboratory focused on human clinical and population genomics, studying the relationship between genetic ancestry and health outcomes. Leads bioinformatics capacity-building initiatives in Latin America and Africa. Director of the Bioinformatics Graduate Program.

faculty.name: Gregory Gibson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneering researcher in systems genetics, studying how genetic variation contributes to complex traits and disease susceptibility. Integrates genomics, transcriptomics, and computational approaches to understand genotype-phenotype relationships in human health.

faculty.name: Peng Qiu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for analyzing single-cell data, with applications in immunology, cancer biology, and systems biology. Creates algorithms for automated cell population identification, trajectory inference, and integrative analysis of high-dimensional cytometry and single-cell sequencing data.

faculty.name: Saurabh Sinha
faculty.title: Wallace H. Coulter Distinguished Faculty Chair and Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computational methods for understanding gene regulation, developing algorithms to model transcriptional regulatory networks from genomics data. Integrates epigenomic and transcriptomic data to decipher the regulatory logic of development and disease.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Skolnick
faculty.title: GRA Eminent Scholar and Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for the Study of Systems Biology. Develops computational approaches for protein structure and function prediction, protein-protein interactions, and systems-level modeling of biological networks. Pioneered threading methods for protein structure prediction and integrative systems biology approaches.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Cherry
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair for Academic Affairs
faculty.summary: Develops and applies computational models to study cardiac electrophysiology and arrhythmias. Creates high-performance scientific computing approaches for simulating cardiac dynamics at multiple scales, from ion channels to whole-heart models, with applications in personalized medicine.

faculty.name: Melissa Kemp
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates computational modeling with experimental approaches to study cellular signaling networks. Develops dynamical models of redox signaling in immune cells and cancer, combining mechanistic and data-driven approaches to understand complex biological regulation.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Markowitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for analyzing large-scale neural recordings to understand how neural circuits process sensory information and generate behavior. Combines machine learning, statistical modeling, and high-throughput experimental approaches.

faculty.name: Christine Heitsch
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies mathematical and computational approaches to study RNA structure and function. Develops algorithms for RNA secondary structure prediction, RNA folding kinetics, and analysis of RNA structural motifs using combinatorial and discrete mathematics methods.

faculty.name: Sam Brown
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational and mathematical approaches to study microbial evolution, social evolution in bacteria, and the evolutionary dynamics of antibiotic resistance. Integrates genomics, population modeling, and experimental evolution to understand pathogen biology.

faculty.name: Patrick McGrath
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates genomics, quantitative genetics, and computational approaches to study the genetic basis of complex behavioral traits. Uses C. elegans as a model system with high-throughput phenotyping and population genomics to understand how genetic variation shapes behavior.

faculty.name: Yuhong Fan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies epigenetic mechanisms of gene regulation, integrating computational and experimental approaches to understand chromatin dynamics. Investigates how epigenetic modifications control stem cell differentiation and contribute to disease processes.

faculty.name: Annalise Paaby
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational and experimental approaches to study how genetic variation produces phenotypic diversity within and between populations. Investigates the evolutionary forces shaping genetic architecture of complex traits using genomics and bioinformatics methods.

faculty.name: Yunan Luo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops machine learning and deep learning methods for protein engineering and design. Creates computational approaches to predict protein function from sequence and structure, with applications in enzyme design, drug discovery, and understanding molecular evolution.

faculty.name: Amirali Aghazadeh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops machine learning algorithms for computational protein design and engineering. Creates methods for exploring the protein fitness landscape, optimizing protein sequences for desired functions, and accelerating drug discovery through computational approaches.

faculty.name: Joseph Lachance
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational population genetics and genomics approaches to study human evolution and disease. Investigates how evolutionary processes shape patterns of genetic variation, with focus on genetic risk factors for complex diseases across diverse populations.

faculty.name: Abigail Lind
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational and comparative genomics approaches to study microbial evolution and ecology. Develops methods for metagenomic analysis and investigates microbial diversity, host-microbe interactions, and the evolution of microbial traits.

faculty.name: Ahmet Coskun
faculty.title: Bernie Marcus Early Career Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for spatial biology, creating multiplexed imaging techniques and analysis algorithms to study the spatial organization of cells in tissues. Applies these approaches to cancer biology and immunology to understand tumor microenvironments.

faculty.name: Facundo Fernandez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mass spectrometry-based metabolomics approaches integrated with computational and bioinformatics methods. Creates analytical and computational tools for high-throughput metabolic profiling with applications in disease biomarker discovery and systems biology.

faculty.name: Lynn Kamerlin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational chemistry and molecular simulation approaches to study enzyme evolution and catalysis. Develops multiscale modeling methods to understand how protein dynamics and evolution shape enzyme function, with applications in protein engineering and drug design.

faculty.name: Kostas Konstantinidis
faculty.title: Carlton S. Wilder Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and bioinformatics methods for studying microbial communities in environmental systems. Uses metagenomics, comparative genomics, and cultivation-independent approaches to understand microbial diversity, evolution, and ecosystem function.

requirements.admission_model: Committee review; recurring Dec 15 deadline (year not posted)

funding.note: PhD candidates typically offered RA/TA with stipend and tuition remission

faculty.name: Mostafa Ammar
faculty.title: Regents' Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on network architectures, protocols, and services including multicast communication, multimedia streaming, content distribution networks, and mobile cloud computing.

faculty.name: Alexandra Boldyreva
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on cryptography and information security, particularly public key encryption methods and provable security, with multiple test-of-time awards.

faculty.name: Moinuddin Qureshi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans computer architecture, memory systems, hardware security, and quantum computing; developed caching algorithms for IBM Power-7 processors and received the 2019 Persistent Impact Prize for phase-change memory work.

faculty.name: Santosh Vempala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include algorithms, randomness, high-dimensional geometry, foundations of data science, and building a computational theory of the brain.

faculty.name: Wenke Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on systems and network security, including intrusion detection, malware analysis, and building practical defenses against cyber attacks. USG Regents Professor and co-director of the Georgia Tech Security Summit.

faculty.name: Calton Pu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on distributed computing, Internet data management, operating systems, and building survivable, scalable systems; holds the John P. Imlay, Jr. Chair in Software.

faculty.name: Ada Gavrilovska
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Kernel research group focusing on performance, scalability, and efficiency across the systems software stack with recent emphasis on edge computing and memory-centric computing.

faculty.name: Vijay Ganesh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of machine learning and automated reasoning, developing SAT/SMT solvers and combining learning with reasoning for trustworthy, secure, and robust AI systems.

faculty.name: Mustaque Ahamad
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans cybersecurity, distributed systems, and dependable computing, with focus on identity and access management and security of healthcare information technology.

faculty.name: Ling Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans distributed data-intensive systems, machine learning, privacy-preserving computing, and large-scale data analytics across cloud and edge environments.

faculty.name: Jacob Abernethy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on machine learning theory, online learning, game theory, and optimization, with applications in adversarial settings.

faculty.name: Joy Arulraj
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on database management systems, particularly main memory systems, large-scale data analytics, and applying machine learning to data management and big code analytics.

faculty.name: Alexandros Daglis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on computer architecture, particularly datacenter and server architectures, with emphasis on hardware-software co-design for emerging workloads.

faculty.name: Hyesoon Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on high-performance energy-efficient computer architectures, GPU architecture, and programmer-compiler-architecture interaction for heterogeneous computing systems.

faculty.name: Qirun Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on program analysis, compiler optimization, and formal methods for programming languages, drawing on computational complexity and graph theory.

faculty.name: Yingyan (Celine) Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on efficient deep learning, AI hardware-software co-design, and enabling AI on resource-constrained edge devices through model compression and architecture innovation.

faculty.name: Alexey Tumanov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on systems for machine learning, resource management, and scheduling for cloud and datacenter environments to enable efficient ML training and serving.

faculty.name: Will Perkins
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on algorithms, statistical physics, and discrete mathematics, particularly the algorithmic tractability of random computational instances and sampling from spin models.

faculty.name: Ashutosh Dhekne
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research bridges algorithms, systems, and hardware prototypes for wireless networking, UWB localization, mobile computing, and IoT sensing applications. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Kuldeep S. Meel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of formal methods and artificial intelligence, focusing on automated reasoning, constrained counting and sampling, and verification of AI systems.

faculty.name: Dana Randall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in randomized algorithms and stochastic processes spanning computer science, discrete mathematics, and statistical physics. ADVANCE Professor of Computing and Regents' Professor.

faculty.name: Zongchen Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on randomized algorithms, discrete probability, and learning theory, particularly Markov chain Monte Carlo methods and their computational properties.

faculty.name: Divya Mahajan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on next-generation sustainable compute platforms for large-scale AI/ML, spanning computer architecture, systems, and databases for end-to-end data pipelines.

faculty.name: Ellen Zegura
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on computer networking and computing for development (ICTD), leveraging technology for social impact. Co-creator of the Computing for Good (C4G) initiative and Stephen Fleming Chair in Telecommunications.

faculty.name: Santosh Pande
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on compiler analysis and optimizations providing deep insights into software behavior, with applications in security, embedded systems, and ML algorithm performance.

faculty.name: Shaowen Bardzell
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Interactive Computing
faculty.summary: Pioneer of feminist HCI and humanistic approaches to interaction design. Co-authored Humanistic HCI and Critical Theory and Interaction Design; researches care ethics in maker cultures and design for women's health. SIGCHI Academy member and ACM Distinguished Member.

faculty.name: Amy Bruckman
faculty.title: Regents' Professor and Senior Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in social computing, studying online communities, content moderation, and collaboration across differences. Author of Should You Believe Wikipedia? ACM Fellow and SIGCHI Academy member.

faculty.name: Munmun De Choudhury
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Social Computing and Digital Mental Health
faculty.summary: Directs the Social Dynamics and Well-Being Lab. Pioneered the use of social media data to predict and understand mental health conditions including depression and suicide risk. Contributed to U.S. Surgeon General's advisory on social connection. Recipient of SIGCHI Societal Impact Award and ICWSM Test-of-Time Award.

faculty.name: Betsy DiSalvo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Culture and Technology Lab (CAT Lab), studying how cultural values shape technology use and informal learning. Designs educational technologies and games to broaden participation in computing, particularly for underserved communities.

faculty.name: Carl DiSalvo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches design at the intersection of technology and civic life, examining how design practices can support public engagement and social change. Author of Adversarial Design. Explores alternative ways of doing design for community-oriented outcomes.

faculty.name: Keith Edwards
faculty.title: Professor and Director of GVU Center
faculty.summary: Researches HCI and ubiquitous computing, with focus on domestic technologies, interactive spaces, and understanding how people integrate technology into everyday life. Directs the GVU Center, Georgia Tech's interdisciplinary research center for people-centered technology.

faculty.name: Beki Grinter
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean for Faculty Development
faculty.summary: Pioneering researcher in CSCW and HCI, studying how people adopt and adapt technology in domestic and everyday settings. Her qualitative research on home technologies, religious computing, and technology in the developing world has shaped the field.

faculty.name: Michael Best
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Information and Communication Technology for Development
faculty.summary: Leads research in ICTD, studying how information and communication technologies can support sustainable development, peacebuilding, and digital inclusion in underserved regions globally. Directs the Technologies and International Development Lab.

faculty.name: Thomas Ploetz
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Researches computational behavior analysis using wearable and mobile sensing. Develops machine learning methods for automated activity recognition with applications in healthcare, skill assessment, and human behavior understanding.

faculty.name: Thad Starner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of wearable computing who has worn a head-worn computer since 1993. Technical Lead on Google Glass. Researches accessibility technologies for Deaf and hard-of-hearing communities including ASL recognition, real-time captioning on smart glasses, and passive haptic learning for Braille acquisition. ACM Fellow and AWE XR Hall of Fame inductee.

faculty.name: Bruce Walker
faculty.title: Professor (Joint with School of Psychology)
faculty.summary: Directs the Sonification Lab, researching how sound can convey information for accessibility and data understanding. Develops assistive technologies for people with vision impairments including audio navigation systems, accessible museum exhibits, and sonification for scientific data. Former President of the International Community for Auditory Display.

faculty.name: Rosa Arriaga
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches human-centered computing for health and well-being, focusing on chronic care management, mental health support systems, and technologies for individuals with developmental disabilities and their caregivers.

faculty.name: Lynn Dombrowski
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches HCI through the lens of social justice, designing and evaluating technologies that support marginalized and underserved populations. Studies how welfare systems, community organizations, and social services use technology.

faculty.name: Andrea Grimes Parker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Founder and director of the Wellness Technology Lab. Researches how social and interactive computing systems can address health disparities in low-SES communities and communities of color. Develops community-engaged health technologies in partnership with churches and grassroots organizations. Joint appointment at Emory Rollins School of Public Health.

faculty.name: Neha Kumar
faculty.title: Associate Professor (Joint with Sam Nunn School of International Affairs)
faculty.summary: Directs the Tandem Lab researching HCI at the intersection of global sustainable development. Designs AI and data-driven systems for frontline health workers in the Global South using ethnographic and participatory methods. President of ACM SIGCHI and NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Jessica Roberts
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches how technology can support learning in informal environments such as museums and science centers. Studies human-data interaction, designing tools that help learners engage with and make sense of data through interactive visualizations and participatory experiences.

faculty.name: Alex Endert
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Visual Analytics Lab, researching how interactive visual interfaces can support human sensemaking and data analysis. Develops visual analytics systems that integrate human reasoning with machine learning for intelligence analysis, text analysis, and data exploration.

faculty.name: Jennifer Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches CSCW and HCI for health, focusing on social health information seeking and sharing practices. Studies how people with chronic and neurodevelopmental conditions use online communities and technologies for health advocacy, empathy, and peer support.

faculty.name: Judith Uchidiuno
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches culturally informed approaches to computer science education for underserved communities in the U.S. and globally. Designs and evaluates learning technologies that are responsive to local cultural contexts and broaden participation in computing.

faculty.name: Cindy Xiong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches how visual representations of data influence human perception, cognition, and decision-making. Studies uncertainty visualization, cognitive biases in data interpretation, and how visualization design can improve data communication and trust.

faculty.name: HyunJoo Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Joint with School of Industrial Design)
faculty.summary: Researches tangible and physical computing, designing novel interactive materials and tools that blend craft, design, and computational media. Develops accessible technologies for creative expression and maker-based learning.

faculty.name: Naveena Karusala
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches human-centered computing for health equity in global contexts, studying the role of care work and community health practices in the design of sociotechnical systems. Uses participatory and ethnographic methods to understand technology-mediated care.

faculty.name: Cindy Kaiying Lin
faculty.title: Stephen Fleming Early Career Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches how sociotechnical infrastructures shape temporal experiences and inequities in public systems such as courts, healthcare, and immigration services. Uses ethnographic methods to understand how people navigate bureaucratic technologies.

faculty.name: Yalong Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches immersive visualization and visual analytics, designing novel interaction techniques for exploring data in augmented and virtual reality environments. Studies how spatial and embodied interactions can support complex data analysis and sensemaking.

requirements.admission_model: Apply through a home school; earliest deadline Dec 1, most Dec 15; fall entry only

funding.note: TA/fellowship guarantees determined by home unit

faculty.name: Jacob Abernethy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on machine learning theory, online learning, and game theory, with emphasis on adversarial settings and connections between learning and optimization. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Yingyan (Celine) Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on efficient deep learning through hardware-software co-design, model compression, and enabling AI on resource-constrained edge and IoT devices. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Ling Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans distributed data-intensive systems, privacy-preserving machine learning, and large-scale data analytics across cloud and edge environments. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Santosh Vempala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algorithms, randomness, high-dimensional geometry, and foundations of data science, with work on the limits of sampling and optimization and a computational theory of the brain. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Alexey Tumanov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on systems for machine learning, resource management, and scheduling, building efficient infrastructure for training and serving ML models at scale. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Divya Mahajan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on next-generation sustainable compute platforms for large-scale AI/ML, spanning computer architecture, systems, and databases for end-to-end training and inference pipelines. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Constantine Dovrolis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research applies deep learning and AI to neuroscience and network science problems, exploring the intersection of artificial and biological neural computation. Housed in SCS.

faculty.name: Sonia Chernova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on robot learning, human-robot interaction, and explainable AI, directing the RAIL lab and leading the AI-CARING institute for collaborative AI systems supporting independent living for older adults. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Irfan Essa
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision, machine learning, computational photography, and robotics, with contributions to video understanding, image-based modeling, and applied ML systems. Senior Associate Dean of Computing. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Judy Hoffman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of computer vision and machine learning, specializing in domain adaptation, transfer learning, adversarial robustness, and algorithmic fairness. Diversity and Inclusion Fellow. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Zsolt Kira
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning for perception and robotics, specializing in continual/lifelong learning, unsupervised learning, domain adaptation, and distributed perception. Associate Director of ML@GT. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Mark Riedl
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on human-centered artificial intelligence, developing ML technologies for story understanding and generation, computational creativity, and explainable AI. Associate Director of the Machine Learning Center. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Alan Ritter
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing and machine learning, enabling machines to read and understand vast quantities of text with minimal supervision, including social media analysis and information extraction. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: James Hays
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision, robotics, and machine learning on object detection, tracking, and scene understanding, with work on novel datasets and self-driving vehicle perception at Argo AI. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Matthew Gombolay
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in robotics, AI/ML, and human-robot interaction, focusing on multi-agent reinforcement learning and explainable AI for human-robot teaming. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Thomas Ploetz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in applied machine learning for computational behavior analysis, developing systems and sensor data analysis methods for automated behavior assessment in naturalistic environments. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Munmun De Choudhury
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research leverages machine learning and social media data to understand mental health and well-being, advancing computational social science methodologies for health applications. Housed in IC.

faculty.name: Polo Chau
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of machine learning and visualization, building scalable, interactive, and trustworthy tools for understanding billion-scale data and ML models, with applications in cybersecurity and AI education. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: B. Aditya Prakash
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in data science, machine learning, and AI with emphasis on dynamical processes on networks, applied to epidemiology, cybersecurity, urban computing, and power grid resilience. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: Srijan Kumar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on AI for security and security for AI, developing methods to detect malicious actors and harmful content online while improving the robustness and trustworthiness of AI models. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: Bo Dai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in deep learning, reinforcement learning, generative models, and machine learning theory, bridging theoretical foundations and practical algorithms. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: Yunan Luo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research applies deep learning to bioinformatics and computational biology, focusing on protein engineering, drug discovery, and learning representations for biological sequences and structures. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: Anqi Wu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research develops machine learning methods for computational neuroscience, building probabilistic models and deep learning approaches for analyzing neural and behavioral data. Housed in CSE.

faculty.name: Larry Heck
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Natural Language Processing and Conversational AI
faculty.summary: Research in natural language processing, deep learning, and conversational AI systems. Rhesa Farmer Chair and GRA Eminent Scholar with extensive industry experience building production dialogue systems. Housed in ECE and IC.

faculty.name: Guanghui (George) Lan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in optimization methods for machine learning, including stochastic optimization, reinforcement learning, and connections between optimization, game theory, and deep learning. Associate Director of ML@GT. Housed in ISyE.

requirements.admission_model: Apply through a participating home school

faculty.name: Frank Dellaert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in robot perception, known for developing factor graph approaches (GTSAM) for simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) and structure from motion. Applies these to mobile robotics, urban scene reconstruction, and autonomous systems. Co-authored Factor Graphs for Robot Perception.

faculty.name: Sonia Chernova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches interactive robot learning, enabling robots to learn from and collaborate with humans. Leads the Robot Autonomy and Interactive Learning (RAIL) lab developing algorithms for autonomous decision-making, skill acquisition, and human-robot teaming in assistive and service robotics contexts.

faculty.name: Matthew Gombolay
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Cognitive Optimization and Relational (CORE) Robotics Lab, researching human-robot teaming with focus on interactive learning, trust, and coordination. Develops algorithms for robots to anticipate human intent and adapt behavior. NIH-funded work on surgical robotics for open-heart surgery.

faculty.name: Animesh Garg
faculty.title: Stephen Fleming Early Career Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches robot learning for manipulation, developing methods that enable robots to acquire complex dexterous manipulation skills. Works on sim-to-real transfer, learning from demonstration, and scalable approaches for general-purpose robotic manipulation.

faculty.name: Sehoon Ha
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches physics-based character animation and robot learning, developing deep reinforcement learning methods for motor control. Bridges the gap between simulation and real-world robotics through sim-to-real transfer techniques for legged locomotion and manipulation.

faculty.name: Harish Ravichandar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches scalable approaches for multi-agent and human-robot interaction, developing machine learning methods for coordinated multi-robot and human-robot teams. Works on foundation models for robotics, task and motion planning, and interactive decision-making.

faculty.name: Danfei Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches robot learning at the intersection of computer vision, machine learning, and human-robot interaction. Develops methods for robots to learn from human demonstrations, understand human intent, and adapt their behavior in interactive manipulation tasks.

faculty.name: James Hays
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer vision with applications to robotics and autonomous systems, focusing on scene understanding, image synthesis and manipulation detection, and learning visual representations from large-scale data for robotics perception tasks.

faculty.name: Irfan Essa
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Researches analysis, interpretation, and synthesis of human activities using time-varying data streams. Works on video understanding, computational photography, and machine perception with applications to robotics, human activity recognition, and autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Samuel Coogan
faculty.title: Demetrius T. Paris Junior Professor and Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches control and coordination of autonomous multi-agent systems, with applications to traffic networks, robotic teams, and cyber-physical systems. Develops formal methods and control algorithms for safe and efficient autonomous operation in complex environments.

faculty.name: Patricio Vela
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer vision integrated with robot control, developing visual servoing techniques and perception-driven autonomy for robotic systems. Works on vision-based navigation, object tracking, and autonomous manipulation using visual feedback.

faculty.name: Azadeh Ansari
faculty.title: Sutterfield Family Early Career Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches micro-scale robotic systems and MEMS devices, developing novel microfabricated sensors and actuators for autonomous micro-robots. Designs and builds insect-scale robotic platforms with applications in environmental monitoring and inspection.

faculty.name: Panagiotis Tsiotras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Dynamics and Control Systems Laboratory, researching control and autonomy for aerial and space robotics. Develops algorithms for UAV guidance, navigation, control, and autonomous decision-making for aerospace vehicles and planetary rovers.

faculty.name: Karen Feigh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches the intersection of humans and autonomous systems, developing cognitive engineering approaches for designing effective human-automation interaction. Works on decision support systems for aviation, spacecraft operations, and complex autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Evangelos Theodorou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Autonomous Control and Decision Systems Lab, researching stochastic optimal control and reinforcement learning for autonomous systems. Develops information-theoretic approaches to control with applications to autonomous driving, agile aerial robotics, and robot manipulation.

faculty.name: Kyriakos Vamvoudakis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches learning-based control and decision-making for autonomous and multi-agent robotic systems. Develops reinforcement learning and game-theoretic approaches for adaptive and resilient control of cyber-physical systems and robotic teams.

faculty.name: Glen Chou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (School of Cybersecurity and Privacy and Aerospace Engineering)
faculty.summary: Researches safe and trustworthy robot control, developing formal verification methods and learning-based planning algorithms that provide safety guarantees for autonomous robots. Works on scalable approaches for verified control synthesis and safe human-robot interaction.

faculty.name: Aaron Young
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the EPIC (Exoskeleton and Prosthetic Intelligent Controls) Lab, researching wearable robotic systems for human augmentation and rehabilitation. Develops intent recognition, control strategies, and mechatronic design for powered prostheses and exoskeletons.

faculty.name: Ye Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Laboratory for Intelligent Decision and Autonomous Robots (LIDAR), researching planning and control for legged robots. Develops whole-body motion planning and model predictive control algorithms for bipedal and quadrupedal locomotion in challenging environments.

faculty.name: Jonathan Rogers
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches nonlinear control theory and its application to robotic systems, including autonomous ground and aerial vehicles. Works on dynamics, estimation, and mechatronic design for high-performance robotic platforms.

faculty.name: Jaydev Desai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Robotics and Mechatronics Lab (RoboMed), researching surgical and interventional robotics. Develops MRI-compatible robotic systems, haptic feedback for robot-assisted surgery, and micro-robotic platforms for minimally invasive medical procedures.

faculty.name: Jun Ueda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches novel actuation and mechatronic systems for robotics, including cellular actuators inspired by biological muscle. Develops precision motion systems and robotic devices for human-robot interaction and rehabilitation applications.

faculty.name: Lena Ting
faculty.title: McCamish Foundation Distinguished Chair Professor
faculty.summary: Researches neuromechanics of posture and locomotion, studying how the nervous system controls movement and balance. Work informs the design of bio-inspired robotic control, rehabilitation robotics, and wearable assistive devices.

faculty.name: Isaac S. Kohane
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Founding Chair of DBMI at Harvard Medical School. Pioneer in biomedical informatics and clinical data science. Creator of the i2b2 platform. Leads the Undiagnosed Diseases Network Coordinating Center. Work on neuropsychiatric disease genomics and research data-sharing infrastructure.

faculty.name: Peter J. Park
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cancer genomics and mutational processes. Work on computational methods for genome and epigenome analysis, somatic mutations in cancer and normal cells. Director of the Park Lab for computational biology.

faculty.name: Tianxi Cai
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School; John Rock Professor of Population and Translational Data Sciences, Harvard Chan School
faculty.summary: Leading biostatistician developing statistical and machine learning methods for electronic health record data mining. Work on predictive modeling, real-world evidence generation, and personalized medicine. Co-director of CELEHS (Cai Lab).

faculty.name: Shamil R. Sunyaev
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in population genetic variation and evolutionary genomics. Work on analyzing patterns of sequence divergence to understand disease risk, human evolution, and the functional impact of genetic variants.

faculty.name: Heng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School; Associate Professor of Data Science, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
faculty.summary: Creator of widely-used bioinformatics tools including BWA, SAMtools, minimap2, and hifiasm, collectively cited over 100,000 times. Leading researcher in sequence alignment algorithms, genome assembly, and computational methods for biological problems.

faculty.name: Nils Gehlenborg
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational visualization techniques for biomedical data. Director of the HIDIVE Lab. Co-founder of BioVis and VIZBI. Work on visualizing heterogeneous data from large-scale cancer genomics studies (TCGA), 3D genome conformation, and EHR data.

faculty.name: Chirag Patel
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational and bioinformatics methods for studying gene-environment interactions. Work on the exposome, integrating EHR data to understand complex disease etiology, and machine learning approaches to environmental health.

faculty.name: Maha Farhat
faculty.title: Gilbert S. Omenn Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leader in translational genomics and informatics for drug-resistant tuberculosis and other infectious diseases. Work on bacterial genome-wide association studies, phenotype prediction from genomic data, and clinical applications of microbial genomics.

faculty.name: Michael Baym
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in microbial evolutionary genomics. Work on visualizing and understanding the evolution of antibiotic resistance. Combines experimental microbiology with computational approaches to study genetic arms races between bacteria and drugs.

faculty.name: Paul Avillach
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in translational bioinformatics focused on integrating multiple heterogeneous data sources (clinical, genomic, environmental) for clinical applications. Work on federated data query systems and clinical decision support.

faculty.name: Pranav Rajpurkar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher building reliable AI technologies for biomedical decision-making. Work on medical AI benchmarks, foundation models for medical imaging, and AI systems that match or exceed physician performance on diagnostic tasks. High-impact across medicine and AI.

faculty.name: Marinka Zitnik
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Rising star in AI/ML for biomedical networked systems. Work on infusing structure and domain knowledge into machine learning for drug discovery, therapeutic recommendation, and scientific discovery. Pioneer in biomedical knowledge graph methods.

faculty.name: Arjun K. Manrai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in machine learning for medical decision-making. Work on quantitative approaches to support physicians, addressing bias and health disparities in clinical algorithms, and translating biomedical data science into clinical practice.

faculty.name: Kun-Hsing Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cancer multi-omics integration spanning genomics, epigenomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics. Work on computational pathology and AI-driven cancer diagnostics. Bridges machine learning, molecular biology, and clinical oncology.

faculty.name: Luke O'Connor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biomedical Informatics, Harvard Medical School
faculty.summary: Researcher in statistical genetics and complex trait analysis. Work on understanding how genetic variation influences gene regulation and complex disease risk. Developing computational methods for integrative genetic analysis.

funding.note: Fully funded (SEAS PhD guarantee: tuition + stipend)

faculty.name: Leslie G. Valiant
faculty.title: T. Jefferson Coolidge Professor of Computer Science and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Turing Award winner (2010). Pioneered PAC learning model, computational complexity theory, and neuroidal model of the brain. Foundational contributions to theoretical computer science and machine learning theory.

faculty.name: Madhu Sudan
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Nevanlinna Prize and ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award winner. Foundational contributions to probabilistically checkable proofs (PCP theorem), list decoding, and property testing. Broad impact across theoretical CS.

faculty.name: Cynthia Dwork
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Invented differential privacy, a rigorous framework for privacy-preserving data analysis. Also contributed to algorithmic fairness, distributed consensus, and the Dwork-Levin theorem in complexity theory. Member of NAS, NAE, and AAAS.

faculty.name: Boaz Barak
faculty.title: Catalyst Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher at the intersection of cryptography, computational complexity, and machine learning theory. Co-author of the widely used textbook 'Computational Complexity: A Modern Approach.' Work on deep learning theory and adversarial robustness.

faculty.name: Salil P. Vadhan
faculty.title: Vicky Joseph Professor of Computer Science and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical computer science, cryptography, and data privacy. Director of the Harvard Privacy Tools Project. ACM Fellow. Contributed to complexity theory, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-preserving data analysis.

faculty.name: David C. Parkes
faculty.title: John A. Paulson Dean and George F. Colony Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Dean of Harvard SEAS. ACM Fellow and AAAI Fellow. Pioneered research at the intersection of AI, economics, and multi-agent systems. Founded the EconCS group. Recent work on AI Economist and AI-mediated deliberation (Science 2024).

faculty.name: Milind Tambe
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: AAAI Fellow and ACM Fellow. Pioneer in AI for social good: applying multi-agent systems and game theory to security, public health, conservation, and social welfare. Director of the Center for Research on Computation and Society.

faculty.name: Sham Kakade
faculty.title: Rampell Family Professor of Computer Science and Professor of Statistics
faculty.summary: Leading machine learning theorist. Contributions to reinforcement learning theory, online learning, tensor methods, and representation learning. Co-director of the Harvard ML Theory group. Highly cited across ML and statistics.

faculty.name: Finale Doshi-Velez
faculty.title: Herchel Smith Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in interpretable and trustworthy machine learning, particularly for healthcare applications. Combines probabilistic modeling with clinical decision support. Pioneer in bridging ML interpretability research with real-world medical deployment.

faculty.name: Ariel Procaccia
faculty.title: Alfred and Rebecca Lin Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computational social choice and algorithmic economics. Work on fair division, computational voting theory, and AI for democratic decision-making. Known for accessible writing bridging CS and society.

faculty.name: Yiling Chen
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Expert in computational economics, social computing, and algorithmic game theory. Research on prediction markets, crowdsourcing mechanisms, and human-AI interaction in economic settings. Bridges economics, AI, and HCI.

faculty.name: Susan A. Murphy
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Statistics and of Computer Science
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow (2013). Pioneer in sequential decision-making and reinforcement learning for mobile health interventions. Developed the micro-randomized trial design. Bridges statistics, ML, and behavioral health.

faculty.name: Michael D. Mitzenmacher
faculty.title: Thomas J. Watson, Sr. Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow. Contributions to randomized algorithms, network coding, hash-based data structures (Bloom filters, Count-Min sketch), and coding theory. Co-author of widely used textbook 'Probability and Computing.'

faculty.name: Hanspeter Pfister
faculty.title: An Wang Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer graphics, scientific visualization, and computational fabrication. Director of the Visual Computing Group. Work spans volume rendering, bioimage analysis, and deep learning for vision and graphics.

faculty.name: Fernanda Viegas
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data visualization and human-centered AI. Co-leads Google's PAIR (People + AI Research) initiative with Martin Wattenberg. Research on interactive visualization for machine learning interpretability and model understanding.

faculty.name: Martin Wattenberg
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data visualization and human-centered AI. Co-leads Google's PAIR initiative. Invented treemaps, collaborated on Many Eyes and TensorFlow. Research at the intersection of visualization, machine learning, and user experience.

faculty.name: James Mickens
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading systems and security researcher. Work on web security, distributed systems, and practical systems design. Known for both deep technical contributions and engaging writing on computer science. Director of the Systems Research Group.

faculty.name: Stratos Idreos
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading database researcher. Pioneer in self-designing and adaptive data systems. Work on learned indexes, data structures, and storage systems that can automatically adapt to workloads. Director of the Data Systems Lab at Harvard.

faculty.name: Minlan Yu
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in networked systems and cloud infrastructure. Work on software-defined networking, programmable data planes, and cloud network management. Award-winning work on efficient network virtualization.

faculty.name: Eddie Kohler
faculty.title: Microsoft Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Operating systems and systems researcher. Co-creator of the Click modular router. Director of Undergraduate Studies in CS. Work on OS design, transactional memory, and reliable distributed systems.

faculty.name: H. T. Kung
faculty.title: William H. Gates Professor of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer in computer architecture and parallel computing. Known for Kung's method in VLSI systolic arrays, the Kung-Leiserson paper on parallel algorithms, and foundational contributions to computer networks and wireless systems.

faculty.name: David Brooks
faculty.title: Haley Family Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer architecture with focus on power-efficient and resilient processor design. Work on voltage scaling, thermal management, and hardware-software co-design for efficient computing systems.

faculty.name: Krzysztof Z. Gajos
faculty.title: Yahn W. Bernier and N. Elizabeth McCaw Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading HCI researcher. Work on intelligent interactive systems, accessibility, and personalized user interfaces. Research on adapting interfaces to individual user abilities and designing AI systems that account for human cognitive biases.

faculty.name: Stephen Chong
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in programming languages and security. Work on language-based security, information flow control, and formal verification for building secure software systems. Director of Undergraduate Studies in CS.

faculty.name: Nada Amin
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in programming languages, type systems, and compilers. Work on mechanized metatheory, gradual typing, and programming language design. Innovator in making formal methods practical for software development.

faculty.name: Alex Szalay
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in data-intensive computing and scientific databases. Architect of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey data system. Leading work on petabyte-scale science databases, spatial data analysis, and applying AI to astrophysics and other sciences. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Steven Salzberg
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computational biologist. Pioneered gene-finding algorithms (GLIMMER), genome assembly methods, and metagenomic analysis tools. Work spans human genome analysis, pathogen genomics, and assembly algorithms. Member of NAI. Highly cited across genomics.

faculty.name: Mark Dredze
faculty.title: John C. Malone Professor of Computer Science; Director of the Data Science and AI Institute
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher. Director of JHU's Data Science and AI Institute overseeing 150+ affiliated faculty. Work on information extraction, sentiment analysis, and applying NLP to public health surveillance including pioneering work on social media-based disease tracking.

faculty.name: Suchir Saria
faculty.title: John C. Malone Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in machine learning for healthcare. Work on probabilistic graphical models, time series analysis, and predictive modeling for clinical decision support. Founder and CEO of Bayesian Health. High-impact translational ML research.

faculty.name: Alan Yuille
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneering computer vision researcher. Foundational contributions to variational methods, shape analysis, and computational models of vision. Recent work on deep learning for medical image analysis and robust visual recognition. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Raman Arora
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading machine learning researcher. Work on statistical learning theory, stochastic optimization, and representation learning with applications to speech and language processing. Focus on theoretical foundations of modern ML methods.

faculty.name: Jason Eisner
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher known for foundational contributions to probabilistic models of language, structured prediction, and the Dyna programming language for declarative NLP. Developed widely-used NLP algorithms and teaching materials.

faculty.name: Philipp Koehn
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in statistical machine translation and author of the standard textbook on the topic. Developed Moses and other widely-used MT systems. Leading research on neural machine translation and multilingual NLP.

faculty.name: David Yarowsky
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading NLP researcher. Pioneered word sense disambiguation methods and cross-lingual NLP. Work on very large text databases, machine translation, and spoken language systems. Long-standing influence on statistical NLP methods.

faculty.name: Benjamin Van Durme
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in NLP and knowledge extraction. Work on scalable semantic processing, event extraction, and building large-scale knowledge bases from text. Contributes to both core NLP and its application to intelligence analysis.

faculty.name: Michael Schatz
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading computational biologist and genomics researcher. Work on genome assembly, structural variant detection, and scalable computing for genomics. Contributed to key human genome projects and developed widely-used bioinformatics methods.

faculty.name: Ben Langmead
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Developer of the Bowtie sequence aligner, one of the most cited bioinformatics tools. Work on efficient algorithms for genomics, text indexing, and high-performance computing applied to biological sequence analysis. Highly cited in genomics and CS.

faculty.name: Alexis Battle
faculty.title: Wu and Zhang Professor of Computer Science; Data Science and AI Institute Director of Research for Strategy and Partnerships
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in genomics and machine learning. Work on statistical models for genetic variation and gene regulation, integrating large-scale genomic data to understand human disease. Builds computational methods at the intersection of ML and genetics.

faculty.name: Gregory D. Hager
faculty.title: Mandell Bellmore Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer vision and medical robotics. Work on computer-integrated surgery, human-machine collaborative systems, and robotic perception. Former chair of CS at JHU. Founding director of the Malone Center for Engineering in Healthcare.

faculty.name: Russell Taylor
faculty.title: John C. Malone Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pioneer in medical robotics and computer-integrated interventional medicine. Developed foundational surgical robot systems. Work on digital twins, human-machine cooperative systems, and medical image-guided interventions. IEEE Fellow and member of NAE.

faculty.name: Misha Kazhdan
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in computer graphics and geometry processing. Known for Poisson surface reconstruction and foundational work on 3D shape analysis, discrete differential geometry, and efficient algorithms for graphics.

faculty.name: Randal Burns
faculty.title: Bill and Lisa Stromberg Department Head and Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Department Head of CS. Leading researcher in large-scale data systems for science. Work on scalable neuroscience imaging infrastructure, redundancy coding for storage, adaptive performance management, and scientific data management at petabyte scale.

faculty.name: Vladimir Braverman
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Associate Head of Faculty
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in algorithms for massive datasets. Work on streaming algorithms, sketching techniques, and their applications to machine learning and healthcare. Contributes to both theoretical foundations and practical deployment of scalable ML.

faculty.name: Michael Dinitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Work on approximation algorithms, network design, and distributed computing. Known for contributions to fault-tolerant network design and metric embeddings. Bridges theory with practical algorithmic engineering.

faculty.name: Xin Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical computer science. Work on computational complexity, pseudorandomness, coding theory, and their intersection with cryptography. Contributes to both fundamental theory and practical cryptographic constructions.

faculty.name: Matthew Green
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptography and security researcher. Work on applied cryptography, privacy-enhancing technologies, and cryptocurrency security. Known for contributions to zero-knowledge proofs, anonymous credentials, and secure systems design.

faculty.name: Abhishek Jain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer. Work on foundational cryptographic primitives, secure multi-party computation, and blockchain security. Contributions to both theoretical cryptography and practical secure systems.

faculty.name: Yinzhi Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science; Technical Director of JHU Information Security Institute
faculty.summary: Leading security researcher and Technical Director of the JHU Information Security Institute. Work on security and privacy of machine learning, web and mobile system security, and payment system security. Bridges systems and ML security.

faculty.name: Scott Smith
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Leading programming languages researcher. Work on type systems, formal verification, and programming language design. Director of Graduate Studies in CS. Focus on making formal methods practical for software development.

faculty.name: Ilya Shpitser
faculty.title: John C. Malone Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in causal inference and missing data. Work on graphical models, semi-parametric inference, and algorithmic fairness. Applies causal methods to healthcare, public health, and social science problems.

faculty.name: Brian D. Davison
faculty.title: Professor and Chair; Daniel E. '39 and Patricia M. Smith Endowed Chair, Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Daniel P. Lopresti
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Interim Dean, Rossin College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Distinguished researcher in pattern recognition and document analysis systems. Work on electronic voting security, cybersecurity, and bioinformatics. Interim Dean of the Rossin College of Engineering and Applied Science. ACM Distinguished Scientist.

faculty.name: Mooi Choo Chuah
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow and NAI Fellow. Leading researcher in network and cyber-physical system security, mobile healthcare, and assistive technologies. Holds 62+ US patents. Previously Distinguished Member of Technical Staff at Bell Labs. Associate Director of I-DISC.

faculty.name: Jeff Heflin
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director, Cognitive Science Program

faculty.name: Henry Korth
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in database systems, blockchain technology, and distributed systems. Work on zero-knowledge proofs, consensus algorithms, and blockchain benchmarking. Co-director of the Computer Science and Business (CSB) program. Co-author of the widely-used 'Database System Concepts' textbook.

faculty.name: Michael Spear
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director of Master's Program

faculty.name: Parv Venkitasubramaniam
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Roberto Palmieri
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in distributed computing and systems. Work on concurrent data structures, transactional memory, and fault-tolerant distributed protocols. Focus on cluster and geographic replication for high-performance and reliable systems.

faculty.name: Eric P. S. Baumer
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in human-computer interaction and social informatics. NSF CAREER awardee. Work on participatory methods for human-centered design of algorithmic systems, natural language processing, and critical studies of algorithms in society.

faculty.name: Hector Munoz-Avila
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Lifang He
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Brian Y. Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; PhD Program Director

faculty.name: Aparna Bharati

faculty.name: David Saldaña
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Sihong Xie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Leslie Kaelbling

faculty.name: Daniela Rus

faculty.name: Antonio Torralba

faculty.name: Regina Barzilay

faculty.name: Tommi Jaakkola

faculty.name: Aleksander Mądry

faculty.name: Kaiming He

faculty.name: Constantinos Daskalakis

faculty.name: Erik Demaine

faculty.name: Piotr Indyk

faculty.name: Ankur Moitra

faculty.name: Samuel Madden

faculty.name: Hari Balakrishnan

faculty.name: Dina Katabi

faculty.name: Tim Kraska

faculty.name: Srini Devadas

faculty.name: Vinod Vaikuntanathan

faculty.name: Yael Kalai

faculty.name: Pulkit Agrawal
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Stefanie Jegelka

faculty.name: David Sontag

faculty.name: Mohammad Alizadeh
