faculty.summary: Research in computer networks and networked systems, including software-defined networking, network verification, and programmable data planes.

faculty.name: Desheng Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in mobile computing, networked systems, and spatial-temporal data mining, with applications in cyber-physical systems and urban data analytics.

faculty.name: Yongfeng Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning, data science, and explainable AI, with focus on explainable recommendation systems, information retrieval, and trustworthy AI.

faculty.name: Lesia Semenova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and responsible AI, focusing on interpretability, human-centered design, and AI applications in healthcare.

faculty.name: Arpita Biswas
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in algorithmic game theory, optimization, and machine learning, with work on fairness in algorithmic decision-making and resource allocation.

faculty.name: Aarushi Goel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cryptography and security, with work on secure multi-party computation, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-preserving protocols.

faculty.name: Fei-Fei Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-Director of Stanford HAI and Sequoia Capital Professor. Creator of ImageNet, the large-scale dataset that catalyzed the deep learning revolution. Research spans computer vision, cognitive neuroscience, and AI for healthcare and ambient intelligence.

faculty.name: Christopher Manning
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Thomas M. Siebel Professor of Machine Learning, Professor of Linguistics and Computer Science, and Director of SAIL. Pioneer in applying deep learning to NLP, co-creator of GloVe word vectors, and leader of the Stanford NLP Group behind CoreNLP and Stanza.

faculty.name: Percy Liang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM). Leads work on HELM (Holistic Evaluation of Language Models), foundation model transparency, and robustness. Recipient of PECASE and IJCAI Computers and Thought Award.

faculty.name: Yejin Choi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dieter Schwarz Foundation HAI Professor and MacArthur Fellow (2022). Research focuses on commonsense reasoning, moral judgment in AI, neuro-symbolic integration, and understanding fundamental capabilities and limitations of large language models.

faculty.name: Dan Boneh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Heads the Applied Cryptography Group and co-directs the Computer Security Lab. Recipient of the 2014 ACM Prize in Computing and 2013 Godel Prize. Pioneering work in identity-based encryption, pairing-based cryptography, and blockchain security.

faculty.name: John L. Hennessy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Turing Award laureate (2017), Stanford President Emeritus, and pioneer of RISC architecture. Co-founder of MIPS Technologies and co-author of the canonical computer architecture textbooks. Chair of Alphabet Inc.

faculty.name: Jure Leskovec
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in graph machine learning and network science. Co-creator of node2vec and GraphSAGE, foundational methods for graph representation learning. Director of SNAP (Stanford Network Analysis Project) and co-author of Mining of Massive Datasets.

faculty.name: Carlos Guestrin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fortinet Founders Professor and Senior Fellow at HAI. Co-creator of XGBoost, LIME, and Apache TVM. Research spans scalable ML systems, explainable AI, and foundation models. Former Senior Director of ML and AI at Apple after Turi acquisition.

faculty.name: Christopher Re
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow (2025). Research at the intersection of ML and data systems: Snorkel for weak supervision, DeepDive for knowledge base construction, and recent work on efficient foundation models (ThunderKittens) and DNA models (Evo, cover of Science).

faculty.name: Emma Brunskill
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads work on reinforcement learning for high-stakes scenarios where data is limited and exploration is costly. Applications in intelligent tutoring systems and healthcare. Teaches CS 234 (Reinforcement Learning) at Stanford.

faculty.name: Chelsea Finn
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the IRIS Lab. Creator of MAML (Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning) algorithm. Co-founder of Physical Intelligence (pi). Research spans robot learning, meta-learning, and developing general-purpose robot foundation models via large-scale imitation learning.

faculty.name: Stefano Ermon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of score-based generative modeling (foundational to modern diffusion models). IJCAI Computers and Thought Award recipient. Also leads AI for social good research using satellite imagery for poverty mapping, published in Science.

faculty.name: Sanmi Koyejo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Stanford Trustworthy AI Research (STAIR) Lab. Develops measurement-theoretic foundations for AI safety and fairness. PECASE and Sloan Fellow. Board President of Black in AI and NeurIPS Foundation board member.

faculty.name: Tengyu Ma
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on theoretical foundations of deep learning, including optimization dynamics, generalization, and robustness. Sloan Research Fellow. Recipient of COLT Best Paper Award and ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award Honorable Mention.

faculty.name: Dorsa Sadigh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the ILIAD Lab. Research at the intersection of robot learning and human-robot interaction. Contributes to Gemini Robotics at Google DeepMind. Leads OpenVLA, an open-source vision-language-action model for robotics.

faculty.name: Moses Charikar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Donald E. Knuth Professor. Leading researcher in approximation algorithms, metric embeddings, and algorithms for massive datasets. Pioneering work on locality-sensitive hashing schemes and similarity estimation techniques widely used in practice.

faculty.name: Omer Reingold
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rajeev Motwani Professor. 2009 Godel Prize recipient for the SL = L result (undirected connectivity in log-space). ACM Fellow. Research spans complexity theory, foundations of cryptography, and algorithmic fairness.

faculty.name: Alex Aiken
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Alcatel-Lucent Professor. ACM Fellow and recipient of the SIGPLAN Programming Languages Achievement Award. Leads the Legion parallel programming system, STOKE superoptimizer, and foundational work in pointer analysis and static error detection.

faculty.name: Monica Lam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Member of the National Academy of Engineering and ACM Fellow. Co-author of the Dragon Book. Pioneering work in compiler optimization (SUIF, affine partitioning) now pivoted to open-source privacy-preserving virtual assistants and LLM-based deep research agents (STORM).

faculty.name: Kunle Olukotun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Member of the National Academy of Engineering, ACM Fellow, and IEEE Fellow. Pioneer of multicore processor design (Hydra CMP) and leader of the Stanford Pervasive Parallelism Lab. Co-founder and Chief Technologist of SambaNova Systems.

faculty.name: Christoforos Kozyrakis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow and IEEE Fellow. Leads the MAST research group and serves as faculty director of the Stanford Platform Lab. Research on cloud-scale infrastructure, systems for machine learning, and using ML to optimize large-scale computing systems.

faculty.name: David Mazieres
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Secure Computer Systems group and co-directs the Center for Blockchain Research. Creator of SFS (self-certifying file system), Kademlia DHT, and the Stellar Consensus Protocol. Chief Scientist at Stellar Development Foundation.

faculty.name: Zakir Durumeric
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Creator of ZMap and co-founder/Chief Scientist of Censys. Conducts large-scale empirical Internet measurement studies to understand real-world security posture. Sloan Research Fellow and MIT TR35 awardee.

faculty.name: Maneesh Agrawala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Forest Baskett Professor, MacArthur Fellow (2009), and ACM Fellow. Director of the Brown Institute for Media Innovation. Research applies cognitive design principles to graphics, visualization, and HCI tools. Contributor to ControlNet for diffusion model conditioning.

faculty.name: Michael Bernstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Senior Fellow at HAI. Pioneering work in crowdsourcing (flash teams, flash organizations) and human-centered AI (Generative Agents paper). Research on embedding democratic values into social media AIs and understanding anti-social online behavior.

faculty.name: Raef Bassily
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of machine learning, data privacy, and optimization. Develops practical algorithms with rigorous differential privacy guarantees; work on distributed histograms deployed in Apple iOS. NSF CAREER Award (2022).

faculty.name: Tanya Berger-Wolf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of TDAI and the NSF Imageomics Institute. Pioneers AI for biodiversity and conservation, co-founder of Wildbook/Wild Me platform for wildlife identification. Elected Fellow of AAAI and AAAS.

faculty.name: Spyros Blanas
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Builds high-performance database systems, optimizing interaction between database kernels and modern hardware. Dissertation work commercialized in Microsoft SQL Server 2014 Hekaton in-memory engine. Google Faculty Research Award and IEEE TCDE Rising Star Award.

faculty.name: Michael Bond
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the PLaSS group designing program analyses and systems for reliable concurrent software. Research spans atomicity checking, data race detection, transactional memory, and information flow control. NSF CAREER Award and ACM SIGPLAN Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award.

faculty.name: Eric Fosler-Lussier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: John I. Makhoul Professor in CSE, directing the SLaTe lab. Works on statistical methods for speech recognition, discriminative language models, spoken dialogue systems, and information extraction from clinical text. Fellow of ISCA and IEEE.

faculty.name: Zeyu Guo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical computer science including computational complexity, pseudorandomness, coding theory (Reed-Solomon list decoding), and algebraic complexity. Previously postdoc at UT Austin, Haifa, and IIT Kanpur; PhD from Caltech.

faculty.name: Pooya Hatami
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on pseudorandomness, communication complexity, analysis of Boolean functions, additive combinatorics, and learning theory. Recent work includes disproving the Implicit Graph Conjecture (FOCS 2022) and results on circuit complexity. PhD from University of Chicago under Razborov and Tulsiani.

faculty.name: Mahdi Khalili
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in theoretical and applied machine learning with focus on algorithmic fairness (individual, group, and counterfactual), privacy guarantees, and efficient ML for resource-constrained devices. Also affiliated with TDAI.

faculty.name: Zhiqiang Lin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor and Director of the Institute for Cybersecurity and Digital Trust. Research spans systems and software security, binary rewriting, trusted execution environments, mobile/IoT security, and AI-security intersections. IEEE Fellow and ACM Distinguished Member.

faculty.name: Xia Ning
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops foundational AI/ML methods for medicine and healthcare including generative models for drug design, LLM-based agents, graph representation learning, and clinical decision support. Division Chief of AI in Digital Health at BMI. Patented EHR AI technology adopted at OSU.

faculty.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs the NowLab and develops MVAPICH (used by 3,500+ organizations in 93 countries). Leads the $20M NSF AI Institute ICICLE. Research on HPC, high-performance interconnects, and scalable deep learning. Fellow of both ACM and IEEE. IEEE Charles Babbage Award (2022).

faculty.name: Srinivasan Parthasarathy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in data mining, graph analytics, and high-performance data analytics. Expertise in building ML/database systems and large-scale network analysis. IEEE Fellow and AAIA Fellow.

faculty.name: Andrew Perrault
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on reinforcement learning and optimization applied to public health, digital agriculture, and community health. Recent work spans RLHF, LLM training and safety (preventing collusion). Postdoc at Harvard CRCS under Milind Tambe.

faculty.name: Feng Qin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on software reliability, storage systems, and operating systems. Known for pioneering work on SSD failure analysis under power faults, crash consistency testing, and concurrency bug detection. Best Paper Awards at SOSP and FSE.

faculty.name: Ness Shroff
faculty.title: Ohio Eminent Scholar and Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the NSF AI Institute on Future Edge Networks and Distributed Intelligence (AI-EDGE). Research on ML-driven network optimization, stochastic control, and communication systems. IEEE Fellow, 2026 INFOCOM Test of Time Award, Editor-in-Chief of IEEE/ACM Transactions on Networking.

faculty.name: Christopher Stewart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the ReROUT Lab. Research on responsive, cost-effective, and carbon-neutral cloud computing systems. Created datagreening.com and was founding Chief Editor of IEEE Sustainable Computing Register. NSF CAREER Award (2014).

faculty.name: Yu Su
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on NLP, conversational AI, and AI agents. Created Mind2Web, the first dataset for generalist web agents using LLMs. Previously senior researcher at Microsoft Semantic Machines. College of Engineering Innovation Scholar.

faculty.name: Huan Sun
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in NLP and AI with focus on LLMs, web agents (Mind2Web, SeeAct), and AI safety for multimodal agents. Leads a $10M AI Safety Science initiative funded by Schmidt Sciences. NSF CAREER Award, best paper finalist at CVPR 2024.

faculty.name: Yang Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in distributed systems, fault tolerance, scalability, and performance optimization. Teaches advanced operating systems and large-scale data processing. PhD from UT Austin; NSF and Nationwide Children's Hospital funded.

faculty.name: Donald Williamson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the ASPIRE Group combining machine learning, signal processing, and hearing science for speech enhancement, separation, and perceptual quality assessment. NSF CAREER Award. Previously on faculty at Indiana University.

faculty.name: Carter Yagemann
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on systems and software security including automated vulnerability discovery, exploit prevention, and Rowhammer defenses. Leads W3CIL Lab. DARPA Young Faculty Award (2024). PhD from Georgia Tech.

faculty.name: Mi Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs AIoT and Machine Learning Systems Lab. Works on efficient AI (multimodal LLMs, diffusion models), mobile computing, and mobile health. 2025 ACM Distinguished Member and National Academy of Inventors Senior Member.

faculty.name: Ting Zhu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in wireless networking, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, and cybersecurity. Work on cross-technology communication (WiFi/ZigBee coexistence) and smart communities. Over 140 papers, NSF CAREER Award (2017).

faculty.name: Xueru Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads ETHIC Lab. Research on trustworthy ML: algorithmic fairness, privacy, robustness, and human-AI interaction dynamics under strategic behavior. Recipient of NSF CRII and President's Research Excellence Award. PhD from University of Michigan.

faculty.name: Zhihui Zhu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theoretical and computational foundations of data science and ML: deep representation learning, neural collapse, in-context learning, and efficient LLMs. Also active in quantum state reconstruction. Area Chair for NeurIPS and ICML. Action Editor for TMLR.

requirements.admission_model: Apply through home department; deadline Dec 1 (Psych/CSHD/Education) or Jan 15 (Computer Science)

faculty.name: Gina Kuperberg
faculty.title: Professor (Dennett Stibel Professor of Cognitive Science)
faculty.summary: Investigates neural mechanisms underlying language processing in healthy adults using fMRI, MEG, and ERP, combined with computational modeling. Also studies how meaning-building is disrupted in schizophrenia, examining abnormal patterns of brain activity associated with language comprehension impairments.

faculty.name: Ariel Goldberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies cognitive mechanisms behind spoken and written language production, focusing on how morphologically complex words are produced and the interplay between lexical and grammatical processes. Uses phonology, phonetics, psycholinguistics, and computational simulation methods. Serves as Director of the Cognitive Science PhD Program.

faculty.name: Stephanie Badde
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Stibel Family Assistant Professor of Brain and Cognitive Science)
faculty.summary: Studies how the brain combines information from different senses, focusing on tactile and proprioceptive localization, multisensory integration, and cross-modal recalibration. Uses psychophysics, computational modeling, eye-tracking, posture tracking, and machine learning to investigate Bayesian perception.

faculty.name: J.P. de Ruiter
faculty.title: Professor of Psychology and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Studies the cognitive foundations of human communication, including turn-taking in conversation, gesture production and comprehension, and the interplay between language production and comprehension in dialogue. Joint appointment in Psychology and Computer Science.

faculty.name: Matthias Scheutz
faculty.title: Professor (Karol Family Applied Technology Professor)
faculty.summary: Studies artificial intelligence, cognitive modeling, human-robot interaction, and computational models of natural language understanding and ethical reasoning. Directs the Human-Robot Interaction Lab and serves as Center Director for Human-AI Interactions at the Tufts Institute for AI.

faculty.name: Remco Chang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies data visualization and visual analytics, developing interactive visual interfaces that help people analyze complex data. Bridges visualization research with database systems and human-computer interaction. NSF CAREER Award recipient and co-founder of two visualization startups.

faculty.name: Michael Hughes
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Wittich Family Professor)
faculty.summary: Develops predictive and explanatory machine learning models using probabilistic methods, Bayesian inference, and variational techniques. Applies these to clinical informatics, including cardiac ultrasound diagnosis and mortality risk prediction from electronic health records.

faculty.name: Jivko Sinapov
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies artificial intelligence and developmental robotics, focusing on how robots can learn perceptual and manipulation skills through interaction with the physical world. Research spans computational perception, robotic manipulation, machine learning, and human-robot interaction.

faculty.name: Robert Jacob
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies novel human-computer interaction modes and techniques, including implicit brain-computer interfaces that use real-time functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) brain sensing as passive input. ACM Fellow and leading researcher in next-generation interaction paradigms.

faculty.name: Paul Muentener
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies cognitive development in children, focusing on early causal reasoning, the interaction between language and causal thinking, and how exploratory play supports STEM learning. Directs the Cognitive Development Lab and uses looking-time measures, action-based tasks, and language analysis.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Race
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the cognitive and neural foundations of human learning, memory, and cognitive control. Uses fMRI and EEG to study how memory flexibly guides behavior, creates predictions about the future, and how these processes are disrupted in clinical populations with memory loss.

faculty.name: Aniruddh D. Patel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the cognitive neuroscience of music, including music-language relations, rhythmic processing, and cross-species comparisons of music cognition. Author of 'Music, Language, and the Brain' and known for the Shared Syntactic Integration Resource Hypothesis linking language and music processing in the brain.

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Jeffrey Foster
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Professor and Chair of Computer Science. Research spans programming languages, software engineering, and security, with work on program analysis, type systems, and tools for building safer software.

faculty.name: Lenore Cowen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor with joint appointments in Computer Science, Mathematics, and Graduate Biomedical Sciences. Research spans discrete mathematics, graph algorithms, and computational molecular biology, with applications to network science and data science. Fellow of SIAM and ISCB.

faculty.name: Remco Chang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor researching visual analytics, information visualization, HCI, and databases. NSF CAREER award recipient (2015). Served as program chair of IEEE VIS (2018-2019) and general chair (2024). Co-founder of two startups (Hopara.io, GraphPolaris).

faculty.name: Matthias Scheutz
faculty.title: Karol Family Applied Technology Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Cognitive and Computer Science and director of the Human-Robot Interaction Laboratory. Research focuses on artificial intelligence, cognitive modeling, human-robot interaction, multi-scale agent-based models, and natural language understanding.

faculty.name: Soha Hassoun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor developing AI and machine learning models tailored for biological data. Research spans ML for systems biology, metabolic engineering, and computer-aided design for integrated circuits, with a focus on analysis and design tools for (re-)designing biology.

faculty.name: Susan Landau
faculty.title: Professor in Cybersecurity and Policy
faculty.summary: Interdisciplinary scholar working at the intersection of privacy, surveillance, cybersecurity, and law. Has testified before Congress on encryption and wiretapping. Author of 'Privacy on the Line' and 'Surveillance or Security?'. Served on advisory boards for NSF CISE and NIST ISPAB.

faculty.name: Liping Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor focusing on generative models, time series learning, and graph learning. Work applies to spatial data modeling, dynamic system simulation, and chemical data analysis. Received NSF CAREER award for contributions to graph generative methods.

faculty.name: Fahad Dogar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor working on improving performance and reliability of networked systems including cloud-based systems, mobile and wireless systems, and Internet architectures. Also designs technologies for developing regions. Published in ACM SIGCOMM, Usenix NSDI, and ACM MobiCom.

faculty.name: Diane Souvaine
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor researching computational geometry, design and analysis of algorithms, and computational complexity. Long-standing faculty member with contributions to theoretical computer science.

faculty.name: Michael Hughes
faculty.title: Wittich Family Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working at the intersection of statistical machine learning and healthcare. Develops Bayesian hierarchical models and variational inference methods, with applied projects in cardiovascular disease diagnosis from ultrasound and mortality risk prediction from electronic health records.

faculty.name: Daniel Votipka
faculty.title: Lin Family Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor researching human factors in computer security, studying the processes and mental models of security professionals performing vulnerability discovery, network defense, and malware analysis. Produces research-based recommendations for education, policy, and automation in security.

faculty.name: Guannan Wei
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on scientific and engineering foundations of programming and software systems. Builds programming languages and tools to help developers create correct, safe, and efficient software. Published in POPL, PLDI, OOPSLA, ICFP, ICSE, and FSE; received ACM SIGPLAN Distinguished Paper awards.

requirements.admission_model: Apply through home department (CS, ECE, or ME); GRE policy follows home department

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Matthias Scheutz
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science; Karol Family Applied Technology Professor; Director, HRI Lab
faculty.summary: Directs the Human-Robot Interaction Laboratory and HRI graduate programs at Tufts. Research focuses on complex ethical cognitive robots with natural language interaction, task-based dialogues, on-the-fly learning, and explainable robot behavior for open-world HRI. Holds appointments in Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering.

faculty.name: Elaine Schaertl Short
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering; CEEO Fellow
faculty.summary: Directs the Assistive Agent Behavior and Learning (AABL) Lab. Develops algorithms for socially assistive robotics and human-robot interaction, focusing on children, older adults, and people with disabilities. Applies human-centered design and disability community values to AI and robotics, with real-world evaluation.

faculty.name: Jivko Sinapov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering; James Schmolze Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Multimodal Learning, Interaction, and Perception Lab. Investigates cognitive and developmental robotics, focusing on how robots can learn sensorimotor knowledge and transfer skills across different morphologies using encoder-decoder and kernel manifold learning approaches. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Reuth Mirsky
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science; Director, GOLD Lab
faculty.summary: Directs the GOLD (Goal Optimization using Learning and Decision-making) Lab. Develops algorithms enabling virtual agents and robots to be effective teammates through goal and plan recognition, negotiation, theory of mind reasoning, and intelligent disobedience in collaborative human-robot settings.

faculty.name: J.P. de Ruiter
faculty.title: Professor of Psychology and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Studies the cognitive foundations of human communication, including language, gesture, and multimodal signals in both humans and artificial agents. Known for research on conversational turn-taking, intention recognition in agent-agent communication, and the JAMES robot bartender social robotics project.

faculty.name: Jason Rife
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Mechanical Engineering; Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic modeling to navigation, safety-critical transportation systems, and state estimation. HRI research includes modeling human perception for search and rescue operations and fusing model and sensor data to track group movements during emergencies.

faculty.name: Chris Rogers
faculty.title: Professor of Mechanical Engineering; John R. Beaver Professor; Director, CEEO
faculty.summary: Directs the Center for Engineering Education and Outreach (CEEO). Pioneers child-robot interaction research studying how children learn engineering and robotics through play. Co-developed ROBOLAB with LEGO, a robotics learning platform used in over 50,000 schools worldwide across 15 languages.

faculty.name: Robert Jacob
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Conducts research in human-computer interaction, focusing on new interaction modes and techniques, implicit brain-computer interfaces, and user interface software design. Brings HCI methodology and perspective to the interdisciplinary HRI program.

faculty.name: Markus Nemitz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leads the Nemitz Robotics Group. Develops 3D-printable soft robots, swarm robotic systems, fluidic control circuits without electronics, and bio-inspired designs (crab, fish) for search and rescue, environmental monitoring, and landmine detection. NSF CAREER awardee (2023).

faculty.name: Barry Trimmer
faculty.title: Professor of Biology and Biomedical Engineering; Henry Bromfield Pearson Professor of Natural Science
faculty.summary: Directs the Neuromechanics and Biomimetic Devices Lab. Pioneers biologically-informed soft robotics by studying how caterpillars control movement without stiff skeletons, applying those principles to SoftWorm robots fabricated from silicone elastomers and shape-memory alloy actuators. Also explores tissue engineering for living robotic devices.

faculty.name: Shafi Goldwasser
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Turing Award laureate (2012) for transformative work laying the complexity-theoretic foundations of cryptography. Co-inventor of zero-knowledge proofs and probabilistic encryption. Also serves as Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing.

faculty.name: Jitendra Malik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Arthur J. Chick Professor of EECS and pioneer in computer vision. Known for foundational contributions including anisotropic diffusion, normalized cuts, and shape context. Member of both the National Academy of Engineering and National Academy of Sciences. H-index approximately 138 with over 150,000 citations.

faculty.name: Ion Stoica
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow and Director of the Sky Computing Lab. Co-creator of Apache Spark, Apache Mesos, and Ray. Co-founder of Databricks and Anyscale. His recent work focuses on AI infrastructure including vLLM and Chatbot Arena for LLM evaluation. H-index of 120+ with over 100,000 citations.

faculty.name: Dawn Song
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow and ACM Fellow recognized as the most cited scholar in computer security. Co-Director of the Berkeley Center for Responsible Decentralized Intelligence (RDI). Research spans AI safety, adversarial machine learning, and decentralized systems. Has won multiple Test-of-Time awards at top security and deep learning venues.

faculty.name: Pieter Abbeel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Berkeley Robot Learning Lab and co-director of BAIR. Pioneered deep reinforcement learning for robotics, including TRPO and Soft Actor-Critic. Co-authored the influential Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM) paper. Co-founder of Covariant. H-index approximately 192 with over 267,000 citations.

faculty.name: Alexei Efros
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Prize in Computing (2016) recipient for groundbreaking data-driven approaches to computer graphics and computer vision. Known for pioneering work in texture synthesis, image-to-image translation, and self-supervised visual learning. Three-time PAMI Helmholtz Test-of-Time Prize winner.

faculty.name: Sergey Levine
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in deep reinforcement learning and robot learning. Developed influential algorithms including QT-Opt, Conservative Q-Learning (CQL), and contributed to the Robotics Transformer (RT-1, RT-2) models at Google DeepMind. Research enables robots to learn complex skills from data.

faculty.name: Jennifer Chayes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Provost of Computing, Data Science, and Society and Dean of the School of Information. Co-inventor of graphons (graph limits) and prominent researcher in phase transitions in networks. Member of the National Academy of Sciences and ACM Fellow. Former Managing Director of Microsoft Research New England.

faculty.name: Umesh Vazirani
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Roger A. Strauch Professor of EECS and Director of the Berkeley Quantum Computation Center. Co-authored foundational quantum complexity theory papers that established the quantum Turing machine model. Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Co-author of the widely used undergraduate Algorithms textbook.

faculty.name: Matei Zaharia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Prize in Computing (2024) recipient. Creator of Apache Spark, the most widely used big data processing framework. Co-founder and CTO of Databricks. Current research focuses on LLM programming models (DSPy), efficient AI serving, and semantic query engines. ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award winner.

faculty.name: Katherine Yelick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow and NAE member. Co-inventor of Unified Parallel C (UPC) and Titanium parallel languages. Associate Laboratory Director of Computing Sciences at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Pioneered communication-avoiding algorithms and self-tuning sparse matrix kernels. Former Director of NERSC.

faculty.name: Sylvia Ratnasamy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow (2025) and ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award recipient (2014) for her seminal work on Distributed Hash Tables (DHTs). Inventor of the Content-Addressable Network (CAN), a core P2P infrastructure. Current research focuses on programmable networks and network functions virtualization.

faculty.name: Ken Goldberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: William S. Floyd Jr. Distinguished Chair of Engineering and elected member of the National Academy of Engineering (2026). Developed the Dex-Net series for deep learning-based robot grasping. Research spans robot manipulation, cloud robotics, and surgical automation.

faculty.name: Marti Hearst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Fellow, ACL Fellow, and member of the CHI Academy. Invented Hearst Patterns for hyponymy extraction, TileBars for text visualization, and faceted navigation (Flamenco). Author of the first academic book on Search User Interfaces. Former President of the Association for Computational Linguistics.

faculty.name: Sanjit Seshia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cadence Founders Chair Professor and ACM Fellow. Co-developer of UCLID, one of the first SMT solvers. Pioneer of counterexample-guided inductive synthesis (CEGIS) and syntax-guided synthesis (SyGuS). Leads research on Verified AI, developing tools (Scenic, VerifAI) for safe autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Benjamin Recht
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher at the intersection of machine learning and optimization. Co-author of the influential paper 'Understanding Deep Learning Requires Rethinking Generalization' and the textbook 'Optimization for Data Analysis'. Known for foundational work on exact matrix completion via convex optimization.

faculty.name: Daniel Klein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leader of the Berkeley NLP Group. Known for foundational contributions to unsupervised language acquisition, syntactic parsing (including the ACL 2003 Best Paper on unlexicalized parsing), and machine translation. Research bridges statistical and symbolic methods for NLP at scale.

faculty.name: Venkatesan Guruswami
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chancellor's Professor and Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing. ACM Fellow known for foundational contributions to list decoding of error-correcting codes and approximation algorithms. Recipient of the Presburger Award and ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award. Editor-in-Chief of the Journal of the ACM.

faculty.name: Jelani Nelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair of the CS Division and leading researcher in streaming algorithms and dimensionality reduction. Known for foundational contributions to sketching algorithms that process massive datasets with limited memory. PECASE and Sloan Fellowship recipient. Also founded AddisCoder, a free coding program for Ethiopian high school students.

faculty.name: Alistair Sinclair
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gödel Prize winner (1996) for pioneering work on Markov chain Monte Carlo methods for approximate counting and sampling. Author of the foundational text on random generation and counting via Markov chains. His recent breakthrough work connects spatial mixing properties to design of efficient approximation algorithms.

faculty.name: Joseph Gonzalez
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Co-director of the Sky Computing Lab. Leads the LM-Sys project including Chatbot Arena and vLLM for efficient LLM inference. Created the PowerGraph distributed graph processing framework. Co-founder of Turi (acquired by Apple). Research focuses on infrastructure for large-scale AI.

faculty.name: Raluca Ada Popa
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award (2021) recipient for pioneering encrypted database systems. Creator of CryptDB, the first practical encrypted query processing system. Co-founder and President of Opaque Systems. Research enables computation on encrypted data with strong security guarantees.

faculty.name: Sanjam Garg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leading cryptographer known for the first candidate construction of indistinguishability obfuscation (iO), a breakthrough result in theoretical cryptography. Research spans secure multiparty computation, functional encryption, and the foundations of cryptographic primitives. Affiliated with the Simons Institute.

faculty.name: Jason Lee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Sloan Research Fellow whose work bridges optimization theory and deep learning. Known for proving that gradient descent converges to global minima in deep neural networks. Research explains why neural network training works well in practice, covering representation learning, nonconvex optimization, and overparameterization.

faculty.name: Jacob Steinhardt
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Schmidt Sciences AI2050 Senior Fellow researching AI alignment and safety. Focuses on measuring and forecasting emerging capabilities of large-scale ML systems, developing robust statistical methods, and ensuring AI systems are aligned with human values. Rising leader in AI safety research.

faculty.name: Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in electronic design automation who co-founded both Cadence Design Systems and Synopsys. His research spans formal methods, contract-based design, and platform-based design for cyber-physical systems with applications in automotive, avionics, and healthcare. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: S. Shankar Sastry
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Thomas Siebel Professor with research spanning nonlinear and adaptive control, hybrid systems, robotics, computer vision, and autonomous systems. Served as Dean of Engineering (2007–2018) and Director of CITRIS. Member of the National Academy of Engineering and American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

faculty.name: Claire J. Tomlin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow and National Academy of Engineering member whose research focuses on hybrid control systems, safe learning in robotics, and air traffic management. Pioneered reachability-based methods for safety verification of autonomous and human-in-the-loop systems.

faculty.name: Ali M. Niknejad
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Donald O. Pederson Distinguished Professor and Director of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center. Specializes in RF, mm-wave, and sub-THz integrated circuits in CMOS and SiGe for communication, sensing, radar, and biomedical applications. Co-founder of LifeSignals and RF Pixels (acquired by Skyworks).

faculty.name: Borivoje Nikolic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow specializing in digital and mixed-signal integrated circuit design and VLSI implementation of communication and signal processing algorithms. Co-author of the widely-used textbook Digital Integrated Circuits: A Design Perspective. Co-leads the SLICE lab for specialized computing ecosystems.

faculty.name: Sayeef Salahuddin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: TSMC Distinguished Professor who pioneered the concept of negative capacitance transistors enabling sub-kT/q switching for ultra-low-power electronics. His work on ferroelectric materials has been adopted in advanced memory and processor technologies. IEEE Fellow, APS Fellow, and AAAS Fellow.

faculty.name: Ali Javey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lam Research Distinguished Chair in Semiconductor Processing working on next-generation electronic materials including 2D semiconductors, wearable sweat sensors, and flexible electronics. Published landmark work on MoS2 transistors with 1-nm gate length. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Ming C. Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nortel Distinguished Professor pioneering large-scale optical switches combining MEMS with silicon photonics, achieving 240x240 switch fabrics. Co-founder of Berkeley Lights, which commercialized optoelectronic tweezers for single-cell manipulation used in antibody discovery and cell line development.

faculty.name: Laura Waller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ted Van Duzer Endowed Professor leading the Computational Imaging Lab, developing methods where optical systems and computational algorithms are jointly designed. Fellow of Optica and AIMBE, recipient of the Adolph Lomb Medal for contributions to computational microscopy.

faculty.name: Alexandre M. Bayen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Liao-Cho Professor working on control, optimization, and machine learning for transportation and cyber-physical systems. Led the Mobile Millennium traffic monitoring project and served as Director of the Institute of Transportation Studies (2014–2021). IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Sanjit A. Seshia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cadence Founders Chair Professor and pioneer in satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) and SMT-based verification for cyber-physical systems, AI, and security. Co-author of a widely-used embedded/CPS textbook. ACM Fellow and IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Robert C. N. Pilawa-Podgurski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Berkeley Power and Energy Center, specializing in high-density, high-efficiency power converters using flying capacitor multilevel and hybrid switched-capacitor topologies. IEEE Fellow with 17 IEEE prize papers for contributions to power conversion.

faculty.name: Boubacar Kanté
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Inaugural Chenming Hu Endowed Chair Professor who invented Berkeley Surface Emitting Lasers (BerkSELs) and demonstrated the first topological laser based on the quantum Hall effect for light. Holds world records in plasmonic nanosensing and metalens bandwidth. Moore Inventor Fellow and Optica Fellow.

faculty.name: John Wawrzynek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: CS Division Chair specializing in reconfigurable computing and FPGA architectures. Key contributor to the Garp architecture (MIPS + reconfigurable coprocessor), BEE2 high-end reconfigurable computing system, and RISC-V ecosystem development at Berkeley.

faculty.name: Clark T.-C. Nguyen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conexant Systems Distinguished Professor and BSAC Co-Director. Pioneer in MEMS resonators for timing and frequency control. Founded Discera, the first company to commercialize MEMS-based timing products, and served as DARPA Program Manager overseeing microsystems programs.

faculty.name: Kristofer S. J. Pister
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: BSAC Co-Director who pioneered the Smart Dust project aiming to put complete sensing/communication platforms inside cubic millimeter volumes. His work on wireless mesh networking and low-power RF inspired the IEEE 802.15.4 standard and Time Synchronized Mesh Protocol (TSMP).

faculty.name: Murat Arcak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: IEEE and IFAC Fellow specializing in nonlinear control theory and autonomous multi-agent systems. Applies control methods to synthetic biology (genetic circuit design), transportation networks, and energy systems with emphasis on compositional analysis and data-driven reachability.

faculty.name: Kameshwar Poolla
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cadence Design Systems Distinguished Professor working on control theory, system identification, and semiconductor manufacturing metrology. Co-founded OnWafer Technologies (acquired by KLA-Tencor). IFAC Fellow and IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Jaijeet Roychowdhury
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow specializing in nonlinear circuit and system simulation and novel computational paradigms. Co-invented Oscillator Ising Machines using self-sustaining oscillators for combinatorial optimization, winning the Bell Labs Prize. Co-founder of Berkeley Design Automation (acquired by Mentor Graphics).

faculty.name: Prabal Dutta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sloan Research Fellow and Brilliant 10 honoree specializing in embedded systems, wireless sensor networks, and IoT. Research spans ultra-low-power computing, energy-harvesting sensing platforms, and cyber-physical system architectures.

faculty.name: Rikky Muller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: S. Shankar Sastry Professor in Emerging Technologies and Co-Director of BWRC, developing miniaturized wireless integrated systems for neural recording, stimulation, and biomedical imaging. MIT TR35 honoree and co-founder of Cortera Neurotechnologies (acquired).

faculty.name: Yakun Sophia Shao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Sloan Research Fellow and Class of 1951 Chair designing domain-specific hardware accelerators for machine learning and emerging workloads. Research spans agile VLSI design methodology, open-source hardware, and heterogeneous computing architectures.

faculty.name: Giuseppe Loianno
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Agile Robotics and Perception Lab (ARPL), developing perception, learning, and control algorithms for agile autonomous aerial robots that navigate using only onboard sensors without GPS or motion capture. NSF CAREER and DARPA Young Faculty Award recipient.

faculty.name: Ana Claudia Arias
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: EE Division Chair specializing in solution-processed electronic materials and printing techniques for flexible large-area electronics. Develops wearable medical sensors, flexible MRI receive coils for infant imaging, and printed biodegradable sensors. IEEE Fellow and NAI Fellow.

faculty.name: Steven M. Conolly
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Montford G. Cook Chair Professor and pioneer in Magnetic Particle Imaging (MPI), having built all MPI scanners currently operating in the USA. Co-founder of Magnetic Insight, commercializing preclinical MPI scanners for stem cell tracking, perfusion imaging, and cancer diagnostics.

faculty.name: Ian Davidson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on human-in-the-loop machine learning, AI fairness and explainability, constrained clustering, and applications in neuroscience. Develops algorithms for fair clustering, outlier detection, and deep descriptive clustering with NIH/NSF/ONR funding.

faculty.name: Hamed Pirsiavash
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans self-supervised representation learning, adversarial robustness and backdoor attacks on deep learning, efficient model compression, and video understanding. Published extensively at CVPR, NeurIPS, ICLR, and ICCV.

faculty.name: Muhao Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the LUKA Lab focusing on robustness, safety, and generalization of LLMs and NLP systems. Works on jailbreak defenses, backdoor detection, access control for LLM knowledge, and privacy risks. EMNLP 2024 Outstanding Paper Award recipient.

faculty.name: Lifu Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on NLP, multimodal learning, and LLM/VLM fundamentals. Works on event extraction with weak supervision, continual learning, and agentic AI for scientific discovery. Recipient of NSF CAREER Award (2023) and ACL 2023 Outstanding Paper Award.

faculty.name: Zhe Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on deep learning efficiency and robustness, LLM applications in recommendation systems, and natural language understanding. Previously at Google. Research bridges foundational AI models with practical interactive AI systems.

faculty.name: Jiawei Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the IFM Lab researching graph neural networks, graph mining, recommender systems, and social network analysis. Published extensively at AAAI, IJCAI, KDD, WWW, and SIGIR. Over 11,400 citations (h-index 50).

faculty.name: Ilias Tagkopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the USDA/NSF AI Institute for Next Generation Food Systems (AIFS). Research integrates ML with genomics, metabolomics, and systems biology for food, nutrition, and health applications. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Jason Lowe-Power
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Lead PI of the DArchR lab and primary maintainer of the gem5 architectural simulator. Research focuses on heterogeneous memory systems, accelerator architecture, and full-system simulation for HPC. NSF CAREER Award recipient with $3.5M+ in NSF/DOE funding.

faculty.name: Mohammad Sadoghi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the ExpoLab researching distributed data management, blockchain consensus protocols, and hardware-accelerated transaction processing. Creator of Apache ResilientDB. Multiple best paper awards at EuroSys and NSDI. Chancellor's Fellow.

faculty.name: Dipak Ghosal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Chair of the CS Department. Research spans high-speed networks, software-defined networking, wireless sensor networks, vehicular ad-hoc networks, and network security. Holds the Tim Bucher Family Chair and Prem Chand Jain Presidential Chair.

faculty.name: Yang Zhou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of systems and ML, focusing on efficient LLM serving, GPU communication architectures (UCCL), and heterogeneous computing. Google PhD Fellowship recipient in Systems and Networking. PhD from Harvard (2024).

faculty.name: Amanda Raybuck
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on operating systems and datacenter systems, including tiered memory management (CXL), energy-efficient software, and zero-copy I/O. Published at OSDI and SOSP. PhD from UT Austin.

faculty.name: Xin Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow researching ML algorithm development for communication networks, healthcare, and food systems. Works on data-driven networking, mobile resource management, and cellular/cognitive radio networks. Co-PI of the AIFS institute. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Matthew Farrens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Vice Chair of CS. Research focuses on high-performance single-chip processors, instruction-level parallelism, memory system design, and interconnection networks. Associated with the UC Davis Computer Architecture Research Laboratory.

faculty.name: Cindy Rubio González
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans program analysis for bug finding, error-handling in large systems, mixed-precision tuning for numerical software, and ML for software engineering. Creator of BugSwarm dataset. NSF CAREER and DOE Early Career Award recipient.

faculty.name: David Doty
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of physics, chemistry, biology, and computation. Works on algorithmic self-assembly, chemical reaction networks, thermodynamic binding networks, and population protocols for distributed molecular computation.

faculty.name: Slobodan Mitrović
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research covers algorithmic graph theory, combinatorial optimization, and memory-constrained computation including parallel, distributed, streaming, and local computation algorithms. NSF CAREER Award recipient. Published at STOC, SODA, and ICALP.

faculty.name: Greg Kuperberg
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Joint appointment in Math and CS. World-renowned for quantum algorithms, particularly the dihedral hidden subgroup problem, quantum error correction, and the Solovay-Kitaev theorem. Research bridges quantum computing theory with post-quantum cryptography.

faculty.name: Jasper Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in theoretical computer science, learning theory, and algorithmic and statistical foundations of data science. Works on ML for optimization under uncertainty. AAAI 2026 New Faculty Highlight. PhD from Brown University.

faculty.name: Hao Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow and ACM Distinguished Member. Research applies statistical ML to security and program analysis including fuzzing, adversarial ML, and software verification. Best known for the MOPS security vulnerability detection tool. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Sam King
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computer security, browser security architecture (foundations of Chrome/Firefox security), and fraud detection (software deployed on 1B+ devices). CITRIS Director at UC Davis. Founded two security startups. NSF CAREER and Navy Young Investigator Award recipient.

faculty.name: Zubair Shafiq
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on making the Internet more private, secure, and safe through empirical measurement. Works on web tracking detection, ad blocking (WebGraph), GenAI privacy auditing, and adversarial ML robustness. NSF CAREER Award and Chancellor's Fellow.

faculty.name: Tapti Palit
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of program analysis, software security, and systems. Automates translation of C/C++ code to memory-safe Rust, develops pointer analysis for CFI hardening, and builds tools for data protection and system call filtering. Joined Fall 2024.

faculty.name: Aditya V. Thakur
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bridges formal methods with machine learning. Researches provable repair of deep neural networks with formal guarantees, automated ML infusion for HPC applications, and scalable program analysis tools. NSF CAREER and DOE Early Career Award recipient.

requirements.admission_model: Priority deadline Jan 15, submissions until Jan 31

faculty.name: Syed Jafar
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in network information theory. Known for seminal contributions to interference alignment and degrees of freedom in wireless networks. IEEE Information Theory Society Paper Award recipient.

faculty.name: Hamid Jafarkhani
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in space-time coding for multi-antenna wireless communications. Known for super-orthogonal space-time trellis codes and contributions to MIMO communication theory.

faculty.name: A. Lee Swindlehurst
faculty.title: Professor (Nicolaos G. and Sue Curtis Alexopoulos Presidential Chair, Department Chair)
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in signal processing for wireless communications, array processing, and MIMO radar systems. Known for contributions to signal processing for multiple-antenna systems.

faculty.name: Payam Heydari
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor (Henry Samueli Faculty Excellence Professor)
faculty.summary: Research in high-frequency integrated circuit design for communication and sensing applications. Focus on silicon-based mm-wave and terahertz circuits and systems.

faculty.name: Jean-Luc Gaudiot
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture and parallel processing. Contributions to multithreaded processors, dataflow architectures, and instruction-level parallelism. IEEE Fellow and former Computer Society president.

faculty.name: Fadi Kurdahi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in VLSI system design and design automation. Focus on system-on-chip architectures, embedded and cyber-physical systems, and reconfigurable computing. IEEE Fellow.

faculty.name: Nader Bagherzadeh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture, parallel processing, and 3D integrated circuit design. Focus on GPU architectures, networks-on-chip, and efficient parallel computing platforms.

faculty.name: Pramod Khargonekar
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in control systems theory. Known for foundational work in robust control and H-infinity control. Recent focus on control and optimization of power grids and renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Ozdal Boyraz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in integrated photonics and optical communications. Focus on silicon photonic devices, optical interconnects, and nonlinear optical signal processing for communications and sensing.

faculty.name: Peter Burke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in nanoelectronic devices and their biomedical applications. Focus on carbon nanotube and graphene electronics for sensing and biomedical diagnostics.

faculty.name: Mohammad Al Faruque
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Resilient Autonomous Systems. Research in cyber-physical systems and IoT with applications to smart grid, autonomous vehicles, and Industry 4.0.

faculty.name: Filippo Capolino
faculty.title: Dean's Professor
faculty.summary: Research in electromagnetics and photonics, including metamaterials, plasmonics, and antenna design. Focus on engineered electromagnetic structures for communications, sensing, and energy.

faculty.name: Aparna Chandramowlishwaran
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in high-performance computing and parallel algorithms for scientific computing applications. Focus on optimizing computational workflows for modern heterogeneous architectures.

faculty.name: Hung Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) for biomedical applications. Focus on implantable sensors, wearable health monitoring devices, and wireless biomedical systems.

faculty.name: Yanning Shen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Machine Learning, Signal Processing, Network Science
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and statistical signal processing for network data. Focus on graph-based learning, high-dimensional statistics, and applications to neuroscience and social networks.

faculty.name: Yasser Shoukry
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in security and resilience of cyber-physical and autonomous systems. Focus on formal methods for detection and mitigation of attacks on autonomous vehicles and industrial control systems.

faculty.name: Marco Levorato
faculty.title: Professor (Joint CS and EECS)
faculty.summary: Research in wireless communications, edge computing, and machine learning for networked systems. Focus on AI-driven optimization of distributed IoT and edge systems.

faculty.name: Boris Vaisband
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in VLSI circuits and systems. Focus on power delivery and clock distribution in 3D integrated systems and heterogeneous integration for high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Zhiying Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in coding theory and information theory. Contributions to coding for distributed storage systems, flash memories, and emerging storage technologies including DNA data storage.

faculty.name: Athina Markopoulou
faculty.title: Professor (EECS, CS)
faculty.summary: Director of the ProperData Center for data transparency and privacy. Research in network measurement, online privacy, and applying data science to understand network behavior.

requirements.admission_model: Applications open early October, close Dec 15

faculty.name: Pierre F. Baldi
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in deep learning and bioinformatics. Research spans neural networks, graphical models, and their applications to molecular biology, drug discovery, and cheminformatics.

faculty.name: Rina Dechter
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (Associate Dean for Research)
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in automated reasoning and constraint processing. Known for foundational work on constraint satisfaction, AND/OR search, and Bayesian networks reasoning. Fellow of AAAI and ACM.

faculty.name: David Eppstein
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Prolific researcher in algorithms and computational geometry. Known for contributions to graph algorithms, graph drawing, computational topology, and recreational mathematics. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Michael T. Goodrich
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer security and algorithm design. Known for work on security-conscious algorithm design, authenticated data structures, and geometric computing. Author of widely-used algorithms textbooks.

faculty.name: Padhraic Smyth
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning, data mining, and statistical pattern recognition. Known for contributions to probabilistic modeling, time series analysis, and applied ML in science and environmental applications.

faculty.name: Charless Fowlkes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer vision and machine learning, focusing on visual recognition, image segmentation, and their applications to biological image analysis and phenotyping.

faculty.name: Sameer Singh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in machine learning and NLP. Focus on understanding and improving large language models, including robustness to distribution shift, interpretability, and evaluation methodologies.

faculty.name: Erik B. Sudderth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic machine learning and computer vision. Focus on Bayesian methods, graphical models, and deep generative models for visual scene understanding.

faculty.name: Sven Koenig
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: Research in AI planning and robotics. Known for contributions to heuristic search, multi-agent path finding, and combined task-and-motion planning for autonomous systems. Joined UCI July 2024.

faculty.name: Alexander Ihler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic graphical models and machine learning, including approximate inference algorithms, belief propagation, and applications to distributed sensor networks and deep learning.

faculty.name: Stephan Mandt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in probabilistic machine learning and deep generative models. Focus on variational autoencoders, Bayesian neural networks, and ML for scientific applications including climate and physics.

faculty.name: Michael Franz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in compilers, virtual machines, and software security. Pioneer in trace-based JIT compilation technology (used in browsers and mobile). Focus on moving target defenses and code diversity.

faculty.name: Nikil D. Dutt
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in embedded systems, computer architecture, and electronic design automation. Focus on brain-inspired architectures, cyber-physical systems, and AI accelerators for edge devices.

faculty.name: Scott Mahlke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in compiler technology and computer architecture, focusing on custom-fit architectures for energy-efficient and reliable computing from mobile to data center scale systems.

faculty.name: Stanislaw Jarecki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cryptography, including password-authenticated key exchange, threshold cryptography, and two-party secure computation protocols. Known for efficient cryptographic protocol design.

faculty.name: Nalini Venkatasubramanian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in distributed and multimedia computing systems with focus on Internet of Things, smart cities, and emergency response systems. Director of the OC Data Science Initiative.

faculty.name: Vijay Vazirani
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical computer scientist. Author of the definitive approximation algorithms textbook. Known for foundational work on approximation algorithms, matching theory, and algorithmic game theory.

faculty.name: Gene Tsudik
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer and network security with emphasis on IoT security, privacy-enhancing technologies, and applied cryptography. Known for contributions to secure group communication.

faculty.name: Sangeetha Abdu Jyothi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computer networking and systems, with recent work on AI/ML. Known for the award-winning study on solar superstorms' impact on Internet infrastructure. Focus on reliable networked systems.

faculty.name: Roy Fox
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in reinforcement learning theory and applications, including skill discovery, representation learning, and RL for robotics. Focus on sample-efficient and explainable RL algorithms.

faculty.name: Paul Dourish
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor
faculty.summary: Leading figure in HCI and CSCW. Research on the social and cultural dimensions of computing, embodied interaction, and the relationship between technology and everyday practice. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Gillian Hayes
faculty.title: Professor (Robert A. and Barbara L. Kleist Professor)
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction with focus on health and education. Director of the STAR Lab, developing technologies for chronic disease management, autism interventions, and educational support.

faculty.name: Andre van der Hoek
faculty.title: Professor (Associate Dean of Academic Affairs)
faculty.summary: Research in software design and collaboration. Investigates how developers design software, fostering creative design through collaborative tools and methodologies. Head of the Software Design and Collaboration Lab.

faculty.name: Yunan Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in health informatics and human-computer interaction. Focus on designing and evaluating clinical information systems to improve healthcare delivery, patient safety, and clinical workflow.

faculty.name: Bonnie Ruberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in game studies and digital culture, with focus on gender and sexuality in games and interactive media. Known for work on queer game studies and LGBTQ representation in digital media.

faculty.name: Daniel Epstein
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction, personal informatics, and ubiquitous computing. Focus on self-tracking technologies, health behavior change, and designing for everyday reflection through data.

faculty.name: Stacy Branham
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in accessibility and human-computer interaction. Focus on designing technologies with and for people with disabilities, particularly for blind and visually impaired communities.

faculty.name: Roderic N. Crooks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of science and technology studies, critical data studies, and HCI. Focus on data-driven technologies in education, digital equity, and algorithmic justice.

faculty.name: Thomas Zimmermann
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering and data science. Known for contributions to mining software repositories, empirical software engineering, and developer productivity. Joined UCI January 2025 from Microsoft Research.

faculty.name: Magda El Zarki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in networking, wireless communication, and multimedia systems. Director of the Virtual Reality Lab, exploring VR/AR technologies for education and healthcare applications.

faculty.name: Joshua Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in automated software analysis, combining program analysis and machine learning to improve software quality. Focus on testing autonomous driving systems and managing software vulnerabilities.

faculty.name: Iftekhar Ahmed
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of software testing, static analysis, and machine learning. Focus on empirical studies and tools to improve software quality in real-world development environments.

faculty.name: Matthew Bietz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Teaching
faculty.summary: Research in human-computer interaction and CSCW with focus on health and biomedical applications. Studying how technology supports collaborative scientific research and healthcare delivery. Joined UCI July 2024.

faculty.name: Aditi Majumder
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics, visualization, and novel display technologies. Focus on multi-projector displays, ubiquitous computing interfaces, and interactive visualization systems.

faculty.name: Gopi Meenakshisundaram
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer graphics and virtual reality. Focus on geometry processing, image-based rendering, and applications of graphics in human-computer interaction and VR systems.

faculty.name: Sam Malek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering with focus on software analysis and testing. Director of the SEAL Lab. Noted for work on software architecture, mobile app analysis, and security for software supply chains.

faculty.name: Mark S. Ackerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in CSCW and HCI. Pioneer in understanding how people share information and expertise in collaborative environments. Studies the social-technical gap in collaborative systems design.

faculty.name: Richard N. Taylor
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus (Director, Institute for Software Research)
faculty.summary: Founding figure of the software engineering program at UCI. Pioneer in software architecture research and the founding director of the Institute for Software Research (ISR). Known for event-based and REST architectural styles.

faculty.name: Andre van der Hoek
faculty.title: Professor (Associate Dean of Academic Affairs)
faculty.summary: Research in software design and creative collaboration. Leads the Software Design and Collaboration Lab (SDCL). Focus on understanding and improving how software developers design and collaborate on complex systems.

faculty.name: Sam Malek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Software Engineering and Analysis Laboratory (SEAL). Research in automated software analysis, testing, and security. Focus on mobile and web application analysis and software supply chain security.

faculty.name: Brian Demsky
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in software reliability, program analysis, and compilers. Focus on automated bug detection, safe parallelism, and building reliable concurrent software systems.

faculty.name: Thomas Zimmermann
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair
faculty.summary: World-leading researcher in empirical software engineering and mining software repositories. Joined UCI from Microsoft Research where he led the Productivity and Intelligence group. Joined January 2025.

faculty.name: Joshua Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Software Engineering, Program Analysis, ML for SE
faculty.summary: Research in automated software analysis combining static/dynamic program analysis with machine learning. Applications to autonomous driving system testing, vulnerability management, and architecture analysis.

faculty.name: Iftekhar Ahmed
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software quality assurance, combining testing, static analysis, and machine learning to detect and fix defects in real-world software systems. Focus on empirical validation of software quality techniques.

faculty.name: Cristina Lopes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in programming languages and software engineering. Known for work on aspect-oriented programming, software naturalness, and empirical studies of programming language usage in open-source ecosystems.

faculty.name: James Jones
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software debugging, fault localization, and software comprehension. Developing automated techniques to help developers understand, test, and debug software systems more efficiently.

faculty.name: Michael Franz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers and software security. Pioneer of trace-based JIT compilation. Current focus on compiler-generated software diversity for security. Fellow of ACM, IEEE, AAAS.

faculty.name: Alexandru Nicolau
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers, parallel programming, and code optimization. Known for contributions to instruction-level parallelism, speculative execution, and compiler-directed parallelization for multicore architectures.

faculty.name: Scott Mahlke
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in compilers and architecture, focusing on generating efficient code for custom-fit architectures. Applications in mobile computing, data centers, and embedded systems.

faculty.name: Debra J. Richardson
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Pioneer in software testing and validation research. Contributions to specification-based testing methods, software process improvement, and interdisciplinary applications of software engineering.

faculty.name: Alexander Veidenbaum
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in computer architecture and compilers with applications to embedded systems. Focus on compiler-architecture co-design, efficient code generation for specialized processors, and power-aware compilation.

faculty.name: Mark S. Ackerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in CSCW with applications to software engineering. Investigates how developers share knowledge and expertise in distributed collaborative environments, informing better engineering tool design.

faculty.name: Ian G. Harris
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in hardware/software co-validation and testing. Focus on verification approaches that span the hardware-software interface, with applications to IoT and embedded system security.

faculty.name: Nalini Venkatasubramanian
faculty.title: Professor (CS)
faculty.summary: Research in distributed and multimedia computing with focus on middleware architectures and quality-of-service. Director of the Resilient and Sustainable Communities Research Center.

faculty.name: Adnan Darwiche
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in automated reasoning, knowledge representation, and probabilistic graphical models. Known for work on symbolic reasoning, tractable inference, and explainable AI.

faculty.name: Amit Sahai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in theoretical cryptography, including attribute-based encryption, functional encryption, and program obfuscation. Director of the Center for Encrypted Functionalities.

faculty.name: Guy Van den Broeck
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on tractable probabilistic models, combining probabilistic reasoning with deep learning. Leading the Statistical and Relational AI (StarAI) Lab.

faculty.name: Songwu Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on wireless networking, mobile systems, and network security. Focus on 5G/next-G cellular networks, software-defined networking, and cloud computing.

faculty.name: Mani Srivastava
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in networked embedded systems, cyber-physical systems, IoT, and mobile health. Leading the Networked and Embedded Systems Lab (NESL).

faculty.name: Jason Cong
faculty.title: Professor (Volgenau Professor of Engineering Excellence)
faculty.summary: Pioneer in electronic design automation, FPGA synthesis, and customizable computing. Director of the Center for Domain-Specific Computing (CDSC).

faculty.name: Miryung Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in software engineering, program analysis, and software evolution. Focus on code refactoring, API migration, and large-scale software analytics.

faculty.name: Yizhou Sun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on mining large-scale information networks, graph neural networks, and knowledge graph reasoning. Leading the Data Mining Lab.

faculty.name: Wei Wang
faculty.title: Professor (Leonard Kleinrock Term Professor of Computer Science)
faculty.summary: Research in data mining, machine learning, and computational biology. Focus on genomics, epigenomics, and integrative analysis of biological data.

faculty.name: Quanquan Gu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
